This role supports the accounting and operations teams by ensuring all completed work is properly documented, billed, and tracked for payment. The role requires strong attention to detail and the ability to coordinate with project managers, field supervisors, and accounting to ensure all work performed is properly billed and documented.



Key Responsibilities

Construction Billing & Invoicing

Prepare invoices for completed work orders, service jobs, and construction projects



Verify that all invoices align with contract pricing, estimates, or unit price agreements

Submit invoices through client portals,
, or internal accounting systems

Maintain accurate billing records and invoice logs

Schedule of Values (SOV) & Progress Billing

Maintain and track Schedule of Values (SOV) for active construction projects



Prepare monthly progress billings / pay applications based on percentage of work completed

Coordinate with project managers to confirm project completion percentages

Ensure billing reflects completed scope according to the contract and approved SOV

Change Order Management



Track pending, approved, and rejected change orders

Maintain a log of all change orders associated with each project

Ensure approved change orders are incorporated into invoices and progress billings

Work with operations to ensure supporting documentation is collected

Billing Documentation & Compliance

Assemble billing packages including:



Work orders

Signed service tickets

Photos of completed work

Change order documentation

Completion reports

Any client-required documentation

Ensure billing packages meet client / contract requirements.

Accounts Receivable Support



Track submitted invoices and payment status

Maintain AR aging tracker

Follow up internally regarding outstanding invoices

Assist accounting with payment reconciliation and billing corrections

Coordination With Operations



Work with project managers and field supervisors to verify job completion

Track labor hours, materials, and subcontractor costs when required

Confirm that all billable work is captured before billing cycles close

Systems & File Organization

Maintain organized project folders and billing documentation in company systems such as:



QuickBooks or similar accounting software

Field service software (Jobber, ServiceTitan, etc.)

Project management tools (
, Asana, etc.)

Cloud storage systems (Dropbox, Google Drive)





Qualifications Required

2+ years experience in construction billing, project billing, or contractor invoicing



Experience working with Schedule of Values (SOV) and progress billings

Strong organizational and documentation management skills

High attention to detail

Excellent written communication skills

Comfortable working remotely and coordinating with U.S. teams Preferred

Experience with pay applications and construction billing packages

Familiarity with change order management

Experience with construction billing software

comfortable using Time Doctor as a time tracker

A reliable internet connection with at least 30mbps and a computer with at least 8gb ram, windows 11 to perform work duties.

2nd monitor is required

title: Video Editor (Performance Ads) - Sport brand
description:
Human Dynamix is a fast-growing performance brand focused on premium knee sleeves for athletes and runners. We create products that help people move better, train harder, and stay injury-free.



We are looking for a video editor who understands performance marketing — not just someone who can edit beautifully, but someone who knows how to create ads that convert.



You will be editing short-form video ads for Meta and TikTok using UGC, product footage, testimonials and training clips.

This is NOT cinematic YouTube editing.

This is fast-paced, high-retention, scroll-stopping ad editing.



What you’ll be doing:

- Edit 10–20 short-form ads per month (15–45 sec)

- Create multiple hook variations

- Add dynamic captions and subtitles

- Improve pacing, retention and watch time

- Test different angles (pain, performance, recovery, stability)

- Repurpose winning ads into new variations



We are looking for someone who:

- Has experience editing performance ads for e-commerce brands

- Understands hooks, retention and direct response structure

- Can work fast and iterate quickly

- Knows Meta and TikTok ad formats

- Has a portfolio of ad creatives that generated results

- Speaks and writes English fluently



Big plus if you:

- Have worked with running, sports or health brands

- Understand athletes / running culture / Hyrox

- Can also give creative input on scripts



This role can grow into a long-term collaboration if performance is strong.

title: VIDEO EDITOR & CONTENT PRODUCER (AI-Powered)
description:
We're looking for a skilled video editor and content producer who knows how to work with AI tools - not just edit footage. This role goes beyond editing. Here's the workflow:

• We record content on Riverside.fm

• You receive the raw file

• You edit, caption, and produce it for each platform

• You schedule and publish it - no hand-holding needed



You'll manage tasks in Asana and take full ownership of the content pipeline from raw file to published post. We need someone reliable, fast, and self-directed - someone who treats this like their own business, not just a job.

This is a part-time role (20 hours/week) with strong potential to grow into full-time based on performance.



What You'll Be Editing

• YouTube Shorts, Instagram Reels, and TikTok clips

• Long-form podcast episodes (audio cleanup, captions, b-roll, chapter cuts)

•
 and Instagram ad videos (direct response style)

• Full YouTube videos - talking head and vlog-style content



Required Skills

• Proficient in CapCut and/or Final Cut Pro

• Strong understanding of short-form content - hooks, pacing, captions, transitions

• Experience editing podcast content (audio cleanup, captions, visual overlays)

• Ability to edit direct-response ad creative - understands what drives clicks

• Comfortable using AI tools - Runway/Sora, CapCut AI, ElevenLabs, ChatGPT/Claude

• Familiar with Asana for task and project management

• Able to receive raw files from Riverside.fm and manage the full edit-to-publish workflow independently

• Reliable internet connection and ability to meet deadlines consistently

• Good communication in English (written)



Nice to Have

• Experience with motion graphics or lower thirds

• Familiarity with real estate investing or B2B marketing content

• Experience with YouTube SEO (thumbnails, title optimization, etc.)

• Experience scheduling content on social media platforms 
, Instagram, YouTube, TikTok, LinkedIn)

• Familiarity with social media scheduling tools (Buffer, Later, Metricool, or similar)



What We Offer



• Part-time, fully remote -  (must be partially available during EST AM Hours) 

• Consistent, stable work with a growing company

• Clear direction and creative freedom within brand guidelines

• Opportunity to grow into a full-time role



How to Apply

To be considered, your application MUST include ALL of the following:

• A Loom video (2–3 minutes) introducing yourself, walking through your editing process, and showing us how you work + explaining why you think you would be a great fit.

• Links to 2–3 samples of your video editing work (YouTube, Drive, or portfolio link)

• At least one sample that includes short-form content (Reels, Shorts, or TikTok)

• List the AI tools you have experience with

• Your experience with social media scheduling and any tools you've used

• Your availability and typical turnaround time for a 5–10 minute edited video



Applications without work samples will not be reviewed. We are not looking for the cheapest option - we are looking for the best fit.

title: Full-Stack Developer (PHP+JS)
description:
Are you a creative and experienced developer eager to make an impact in a fast-paced, high-performance environment? At HelloPrint, we’re revolutionizing online printing by developing innovative solutions that make ordering easy worldwide.



As a Full-Stack Developer, you’ll collaborate with a driven and diverse team, pushing the boundaries of technology and building features that elevate the HelloPrint experience. This role offers the chance to grow, innovate, and thrive in our vibrant and inclusive culture.



What You’ll Do:

- Develop & Innovate: Design and build impactful features that enhance HelloPrint’s products and user experience.

- Collaborate & Create: Work closely with multidisciplinary teams, including developers, designers, and product owners, to bring ideas to life.

- Drive Excellence: Continuously improve processes, tools, and systems to ensure high-quality outcomes.

- Learn & Share: Stay ahead of industry trends and share insights to foster growth and knowledge within the team.

- Embody Our Culture: Live our core values, including ownership, raising the bar, and doing it together, to deliver exceptional results.



Job requirements

What You’ll Bring:

- Expertise in Full-Stack Development: 7+ years of experience working with PHP and JavaScript frameworks (preferably Vue.js).

- Creative Problem-Solving: A pragmatic approach and a passion for finding innovative solutions.

- Collaborative Spirit: Strong communication skills and a team-first mindset.

- Curiosity & Adaptability: A hunger for learning and the ability to thrive in a dynamic, fast-changing environment.

- Results-Driven Approach: A focus on delivering high-quality outcomes and making an impact.



Bonus Points If You Have:

- Background in startups or high-growth environments.

- A track record of leading small teams or mentoring peers.



If you're interested, apply here:

title: Senior Backend PHP Engineer
description:
We’re looking for a Senior Backend PHP Engineer to help design and build the scalable backbone of HelloPrint’s ecommerce platforms. You’ll work on microservices, APIs, and integrations, while mentoring others and driving technical excellence.



You’ll take a leading role in shaping the architecture of our ecommerce infrastructure. You’ll build reliable APIs, optimize performance, and ensure our systems scale as we expand globally. Beyond coding, you’ll mentor teammates, influence technical direction, and contribute to a high-performance engineering culture.



What You’ll Do:



- Architect Our API-First Future: Design, develop, and maintain a robust and high-performance API layer that will serve as the core of our headless platform.



- Own the Backend: Take full ownership of key backend services, from design to deployment, ensuring they align with our API-first strategy.



- Drive Modernization: Lead the modernization of our existing system, with a clear focus on decoupling the frontend from the backend and creating a clean, API-first architecture. We prefer a pragmatic approach over the complexity and overhead of microservices.



- Mentor and Lead: Guide and mentor other backend developers, sharing your expertise in API design, performance, and best practices.



- Collaborate and Innovate: Work closely with frontend engineers, product managers, and business stakeholders to translate ambitious goals into practical, well-engineered API specifications and solutions.



- Champion Best Practices: Introduce and advocate for modern API development tools, standards, and methodologies (e.g., OpenAPI, CI/CD, comprehensive testing).



Requirements

What We’re Looking For:



- Proven Seniority: At least 5+ years of professional experience in backend development, with a strong focus on PHP.



- API-First Mindset: Demonstrated experience designing and building scalable, RESTful or GraphQL APIs. You have a deep understanding of what it means to build a headless system.



- PHP8 Expertise: Deep, hands-on experience with modern PHP, including PHP8 and modern design patterns like TDD. You are comfortable with frameworks like Symfony and understand the nuances of the PHP ecosystem.



- Strategic & Pragmatic: You are a problem-solver who prioritizes impact and understands the challenges of a large-scale modernization project. You know when to refactor and when to build new, all while avoiding unnecessary complexity.



- Ownership Mentality: You are proactive about identifying and fixing issues and feel a strong sense of responsibility for the performance and health of the codebase.



- Data Savvy: Strong experience with relational databases (MySQL/MariaDB) and a solid understanding of database design, optimization, and query performance in an API context.



- Experience with Git and agile methodologies (Scrum, Kanban).



- AI-Accelerated Development: You are knowledgeable about and in favor of using AI to accelerate the software development lifecycle. You're keen on exploring how AI can be leveraged to generate code, improve quality, and maximize output.





Nice to Have:



- Experience with e-commerce or high-traffic consumer environments.

- Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP) and containerization (Docker, Kubernetes).

- Experience with message queues (e.g., GCP PubSub) or other asynchronous processing technologies.



What We Offer:

- Growth & Ownership: A performance culture where you grow fast, take ownership early, and design your own path: with access to AI learning and real responsibility.

- International Experience: Join a diverse team of 150+ HelloPrinters from 34+ nationalities, collaborating across countries, cultures, and functions.

- Impact & Innovation: Work in a fast-paced, tech-driven scale-up where your ideas shape the future of customised products.

- Collaboration: Learn from experienced leaders, contribute to meaningful projects, and thrive in a team that’s serious about performance and people.

- Celebrations & Culture: From work anniversaries and team events to monthly Keynotes and seasonal awards, we make time to celebrate wins and give back through volunteering.



Ready to Build the Future?

We are looking for bold thinkers, decisive executors, and engineers who embrace AI-first development.



If you're interested, apply here:

title: Part-Time Virtual Assistant — Squarespace, Facebook & Admin
description:
Virtual Assistant — Part-Time

Naomi Rose | 
 | Oakland, CA (Remote) — approximately 5–8 hours/week to start



About This Work

I am a Book Developer and Creative Midwife — I help writers, healers, and spiritual seekers bring their books into being. This involves 1:1 client work, products for sale from my website, and an online group called “The Co-Writing Sanctuary.” My work has a specific aesthetic: unhurried, sanctuary-like, depth-honoring. I am also ordained as a Sufi healer, so my actual work and my website and
 Business page should reflect this as well as my aesthetic.



My website (
),
 Business page for the Co-Writing Sanctuary, and Heallist practitioner profile (Heallist is a booking site for healers and people seeking healers) all carry this tone and feeling.



I’m looking for a detail-oriented, aesthetically sensitive VA who can implement my vision accurately and reliably — someone who will ask clarifying questions before acting, rather than making independent judgment calls on anything outward-facing. All copy and voice decisions remain mine. Your role is careful, faithful implementation.



What You’ll Be Doing

(1) Squarespace website implementation (I use 7.1):

• Implement page structure and navigation changes following a detailed blueprint I provide

• Enter approved copy (written by me) into pages — no independent writing

• Before making any changes: archive original pages to “Not Linked” status as a safety backup so nothing is lost

• Alt text for images: I already have a detailed Word document with alt text and descriptions for almost all existing site images; the task here is minimal — adding alt text only for any images not yet covered

• Convert the current “Deep Writing Blog” from individual regular pages to a proper Squarespace Blog page type; migrate all existing essays in as blog posts, keeping the same titles and content; rename the nav item as directed

• Check and correct mobile view across all pages

• Remove outdated elements and implement structural updates per my blueprint



(2)
 Business page (Co-Writing Sanctuary):

The page exists with a profile image and cover photo already in place. The current need is visibility — getting people to know the page exists and inviting them to follow it.

• Send personal invitations to past Co-Writing Sanctuary participants using warm templates I write

• Use my
 list (which I will provide) to reach out to others and invite them to the
 page

• Build and maintain an invitation tracking spreadsheet

• Research relevant
 groups (women over 50, women writers, spiritual seekers, healers-who-write, conscious aging, etc.) — cross-referencing suggestions I provide — and compile a curated list with notes on each group’s size, tone, activity level, and whether they allow boosted posts, for my review and decision

• Monitor the page for comments or messages and flag anything needing my personal response



(3) Heallist practitioner profile:

• Complete the profile setup following Heallist’s framework, and copy that I will supply

• Set up my Discovery Call booking flow, connecting my Discovery Call invitations on my website to my Heallist booking page

• Verify that all offerings are listed correctly with accurate pricing

• Test the booking experience from a user’s perspective and report any issues



(4) General administrative support:

• Client list organization: I have past client information in
s and Dropbox files; help me compile and organize this into a clean, segmented spreadsheet (at minimum: past clients / people who inquired but didn’t work with me / other contacts)

•
 platform setup: research and recommend a simple, affordable
 service (such as Mailchimp or similar) appropriate for a small list; help set it up so that bulk outreach goes through a proper platform rather than my personal Yahoo account — this protects deliverability and keeps
s from going to spam

• Other implementation tasks as they arise — always with clear direction fro



(5) What This Role Is Not

This is not a content creation or copywriting role. I write all outward-facing copy myself. I am not looking for someone to generate posts, write website text, or create marketing materials. I need someone who implements my vision faithfully and carefully — a skilled executor, not a creative decision-maker.

What I’m Looking For

• Solid Squarespace experience — editing pages, managing navigation, converting page types, and checking mobile view

•
 Business Manager experience — setting up and managing Business pages, understanding how to research groups and boost posts

• Basic
 platform familiarity — Mailchimp or similar; able to set up a list, import contacts, and configure basic settings

• Strong attention to aesthetic detail — you will be working within an established visual and tonal brand, keeping true to that aesthetic

• Excellent written English — for clear communication with me and for reading and implementing my copy accurately

• A working style where you ask before acting — especially on anything that will be visible to the public

• Comprehension about what you’re asked to do, whether before, during, or after carrying out a task — this means checking in with me as needed if something is unclear, so I can clarify

• Reliability and follow-through — I need to be able to trust that what I’ve asked you for has been completed with care and attention



(6) Practical Details

• Approximately 5–8 hours per week to start, with potential to grow. 

• Compensation: $7–10/hour USD, commensurate with experience

• Fully remote; I am based in Oakland, CA (Pacific Time)

• Flexible scheduling — we can discuss what works for you and your time zone

• Communication primarily by
; occasional brief check-in calls as needed



(7) How to Apply

Please send a personalized message (not a form letter) that tells me:

• What draws you to this particular role

• What you see as your most relevant skills

• Your experience with Squarespace — specifically, what kinds of changes you’ve made to other clients’ Squarespace sites

• Your experience with
 Business pages, and the scope you have addressed

• A brief example of a project where you worked carefully within someone else’s established aesthetic or brand (please include the URL if available)

• Is there anything else you’d like me to know?

• Please complete the brief skills assessment linked here, and submit it along with your cover letter: 
. Applications received without the completed assessment will not be reviewed."



I read every thoughtful application with care. A personal letter — one that shows you’ve read this posting and considered whether this work is genuinely a fit for you — will always stand out.



I look forward to hearing from you.

title: Part-Time Shopify / Funnelish Developer & Designer
description:
I run a performance marketing company focused on health and consumer products. We drive traffic through paid ads and convert through high-performing sales funnels and Shopify stores.



I handle strategy, ad creative, and copywriting. What I need is a skilled developer/designer who can take my ideas, reference pages, and wireframes and turn them into polished, conversion-optimized stores and funnels.



You'll be working directly with me. I've been in this business for over 10 years and am currently based in Asia. I also enjoy traveling to the Philippines!



What You'll Be Doing



- Building and customizing Shopify stores (themes, product pages, landing pages)

- Creating high-converting Funnelish funnels (checkout flows, upsells, downsells, order bumps)

- Writing custom HTML/CSS sections for Funnelish when the builder's native elements aren't enough

- Replicating designs from reference pages I provide and adapting them for our products

- Making everything fully responsive and mobile-optimized

- Connecting integrations: Shopify to Funnelish product sync, payment gateways, fulfillment (CJ Dropshipping, etc.)

- Ongoing tweaks, A/B test variations, and design updates as we scale winners



What I'm Looking For



- **Shopify experience is required.** Theme customization, Liquid basics, product page design. You should be able to look at a top-performing store and recreate that level of quality.

- **Funnelish experience is a strong plus.** If you don't know Funnelish yet but have experience with ClickFunnels, GoHighLevel, or similar funnel builders, you'll pick it up fast.

- **Solid HTML/CSS skills.** Many of our best-performing sections are custom coded. You need to be comfortable writing clean, responsive HTML/CSS from scratch or from a reference.

- **Eye for design.** I'll give you examples and direction, but you should understand what makes a page look professional and convert well. Spacing, typography, hierarchy, trust elements.

- **Direct response mindset.** You understand that these pages exist to sell. You know why urgency bars, social proof, comparison tables, and benefit-driven layouts matter.

- **Works independently.** I'll give you briefs, reference links, and screenshots. You deliver polished work without needing hand-holding.

- **Communicates clearly.** Updates on progress, flags issues early, asks smart questions.



Software You Should Know



- Shopify Admin & Theme Editor (Dawn, Debut, or custom themes)

- Funnelish (or willingness to learn quickly)

- HTML, CSS, basic JavaScript

- Canva or Figma (for quick mockups or asset creation)

- Google Drive / Slack for workflow and communication



Nice to Have (Not Required)



- Experience with Shopify Liquid templating

- Familiarity with CJ Dropshipping or other fulfillment integrations

- Basic graphic design skills (badges, icons, trust seals)

- Experience with conversion rate optimization or split testing



What's In It for You

To Apply

4. **Your monthly rate expectation and availability.** Will pay more for someone with impressive work and strong attention to detail.

title: Remote Appointment Setter
description:
Position Overview:



Apply here







At Ready Roles, we don’t just fill positions—we build lasting, impactful careers. As a Remote Data Activator, you’ll serve as a dedicated sales support engine for U.S.-based moving companies by unlocking revenue from untapped seller data provided by USA Home Listings (Tier 2). This is not an admin or back-office role—your job is to drive action and revenue through strategic outbound communication.



The Data Activator’s core mission is simple: turn overlooked homeowner data into booked moving estimates and referral opportunities. You’ll use a proven outreach playbook to connect with sellers, engage realtors, and drive measurable value directly into your client’s sales pipeline.







Why Join Ready Roles?



A Career Path, Not Just a Job: This role is designed for growth-minded professionals who want to contribute directly to revenue outcomes and be seen as an essential part of a business’s sales process.



Independence and Opportunity to Lead: You’ll take full ownership of daily outreach and play a pivotal role in your client’s lead flow. Your job is to work the data like it’s your own book of business.



Respectful, Growth-Focused Clients: You’ll support clients who are ready to invest in outbound efforts and value performance-driven tea
mbers who help them win.







Key Responsibilities:



1. Lead Activation & Seller Outreach



Conduct daily outbound texting to homeowners using USA Home Listings (Tier 2 data).

Follow up persistently and professionally to book estimates for the moving company.

Monitor seller responsiveness and tailor outreach cadence to maximize conversion.



2. Realtor Engagement & Referral Building



Reach out to local realtors in the client’s market to set up referral introductions.

Track realtor interactions and maintain a consistent pipeline of new connection opportunities.



3. Data Cleaning & Filtering



Proactively sort and remove leads that are no longer active or valid (e.g., homes already empty or sold).

Maintain a clean, focused lead list for consistent outbound activity.



4. Booking & Scheduling



Input booked estimates directly into the client’s CRM or calendar, following their specific process.

Ensure appointment details are accurate and the client is notified promptly.



5. Tracking & Reporting



Log all activity—outbound messages, booked calls, realtor outreach, and removals—in a Google Sheet, CRM, or client platform.

Provide clear, consistent updates on progress and performance.





What We’re Looking For:



Self-Driven, Proactive Mindset - This role is for someone who naturally takes the initiative and actively finds ways to contribute. You’ll be expected to add value without waiting for instructions and to be a driving force for the business.

Commitment to Long-Term Growth - Ready Roles is built on loyalty and stability. We’re looking for someone who values a long-term commitment, seeks stability, and is motivated to build a future with us and the business owner they support.

Aligned with Our Values - Ready Roles believes in accountability, respect, and integrity. We’re looking for someone who takes ownership of their work, consistently shows up with purpose, and is eager to create a lasting impact.





Qualifications:



Must be based in the Philippines

18 years old and above

Willing to work from home with a stable internet connection

Must have own equipment — reliable laptop or PC and a noise-cancelling headset

Available to work full-time (40 hours per week)

At least 1 year of experience in a sales support, lead generation, or customer-facing VA role.

Strong written English communication skills with a clear, professional tone.

Proven ability to work through large datasets and prioritize high-value leads.

Familiarity with outbound messaging, CRM tools, and scheduling software.

Organized and self-motivated with a strong sense of accountability.

A quick learner who can follow scripts, iterate when needed, and identify high-converting patterns.

Self-Driven, Proactive Mindset – This role is for someone who naturally takes the initiative and actively finds ways to contribute. You’ll be expected to add value without waiting for instructions.

Commitment to Long-Term Growth – Ready Roles is built on loyalty and stability. We’re looking for someone who values a long-term commitment, seeks stability, and is motivated to grow with the business they support.

Aligned with Our Values – We believe in accountability, respect, and integrity. We’re looking for someone who takes ownership of their work and drives results.





What You Can Expect from Ready Roles:



Clear Growth Path and Scalable Compensation: As the business grows and you take on additional responsibilities, the business owner is committed to scaling your compensation. This means your role and pay will increase as the team expands and your impact grows.



Supportive, Stable Environment: Ready Roles partners with respectful, growth-focused clients who are committed to creating a positive, long-term working relationship with you. Our goal is to set you up for success so that you can help the business owner achieve their vision.



Training and Development: Continuous learning and access to resources that help you become an indispensable part of the client’s team. Our onboarding process and ongoing support ensure you have the foundation needed to succeed and grow.







Why Our Clients Depend on Us



Our clients are ambitious business owners who trust Ready Roles to help them grow and succeed. They count on a reliable, proactive partner who can stay one step ahead, push organizational goals, and add lasting value. We’re looking for someone who is motivated by the opportunity to build, lead, and grow, and who values the chance to make a true difference in a business owner’s life.







Apply Today



If you’re ready to commit, lead, and build a career with purpose, stability, and growth, we’d love to hear from you. Apply today to join Ready Roles and start building a future where your work drives real impact, stability, and lasting success..

title: Shopify Product & Pricing Specialist
description:
We are a premium sneaker resell company based in France, selling limited sneakers and

streetwear

through Shopify and retail. We are looking for a reliable Shopify Product & Pricing Specialist

for long-term collaboration.



Main Responsibilities – Product Creation

• Create SEO titles, meta descriptions, optimized URLs

• Add correct variants (size, color)

• Add materials and specifications

Image Optimization (Photoshop Required)

• Basic retouching (brightness, contrast)

• Compress images for fast loading

• Rename files with SEO structure

Dynamic Pricing Management



• Update prices based on market fluctuations

• Apply rounding and minimum margin rules

• Use Excel for calculations



• Import/export CSV files

• Double-check pricing accuracy

• Report pricing anomalies



Excel & Data Management



• Advanced Excel (VLOOKUP/XLOOKUP, formulas)

• Data cleaning

• SKU matching

• Margin verification (cost vs selling price)



Ongoing Optimization



• Improve product titles for SEO

• Standardize product structures

• Maintain clean catalog

• Archive sold-out products



Requirements



• Strong Shopify experience

• Advanced Excel skills

• Basic Photoshop skills

• Attention to detail

• Understanding of profit margins

• Fluent in English



KPIs



• Low error rate

• Fast product uploads

• Respect of margin rules

• High SEO quality

• Low revision requests



Application Questions



1. How many Shopify products have you uploaded?

3. Experience with dynamic pricing?

4. Share 2 Shopify stores you worked on.

5. Your hourly rate?

title: Graphic Designer with Branding Experience
description:
About Us

X-Brands builds and scales brands through smart design, marketing, and innovation. We work with multiple clients to create distinct brand identities, high-performing e-commerce assets, and compelling visual systems.

We’re growing fast and looking for a Creative Designer who can think strategically about branding, execute clean designs, and confidently use modern tools (including AI) to bring ideas to life.





What You’ll Do



Create and evolve brand identities for multiple clients (logos, color systems, typography, visual language).

Design websites and landing pages from a UI/UX and visual design perspective 

Design Amazon A+ content and storefronts that are visually strong and conversion-focused.

Use AI tools (ChatGPT, image generators, design assistants) to ideate, iterate, and accelerate creative output—while maintaining quality and originality.

Design packaging and product visuals that stand out both online and on shelves.

(Bonus) Support photo editing, retouching, or light motion/animation when needed.





What We’re Looking For

Strong Amazon design experience (A+ content, storefronts, e-commerce visuals).

Proven ability to create brand identities, not just individual assets.

Comfortable working across multiple brands and clients with different aesthetics.

Confident using AI/ChatGPT in your creative workflow and able to explain how you use it.

A strong portfolio showing branding, e-commerce, advertising, or packaging work.

Proficiency in Photoshop, Illustrator, and other design tools you rely on.

Detail-oriented, fast, collaborative, and able to handle feedback well.

(Nice to have) Photo editing, motion design, or short-form video experience.





The Process



Submit your portfolio (required).

Shortlisted candidates will complete a short creative test





Why Join Us



Shape multiple brands from the ground up.

Work in an environment that values creative thinking and experimentation.

Use creativity + AI to produce work that actually performs.

See your designs make a real, measurable impact across brands.

title: SEO Link Building Specialist
description:

The right person is more important than years of experience, but you do need to have some existing skills and SEO knowledge so you already know you enjoy it and I don't have to start the teaching process from scratch.

mbers, a technical specialist and a content writer, and I'm looking for this person to complete my team.

-
 outreach for backlink opportunities

ing and communicating with people to obtain new backlinks, you will also be creating profiles and guest blog posts. You also must be able to deal with the repetitive nature of link building.

Thanks

title: Hubspot Expert
description:
Ultra Labs is searching for a proactive, highly organized Marketing Manager to support and strengthen our marketing operations across multiple projects and brands. Ultra Labs is a U.S.–based Business Incubator/Accelerator focused on software and tech-driven businesses.



We are searching for a proactive, highly organized HubSpot Expert to support and strengthen our marketing operations across multiple projects and brands. This role goes beyond platform management. You'll take ownership of our HubSpot ecosystem, ensure smooth execution across campaigns, and support data-driven decision making for a portfolio of fast-moving businesses.



The ideal candidate is independent, detail-oriented, and highly accountable, with a strong command of HubSpot's CRM, workflow automation, and reporting capabilities. If you thrive in a dynamic, multi-brand environment and love turning messy processes into clean, scalable systems, we'd love to hear from you.



Key Responsibilities

_CRM Management: Own and maintain the HubSpot CRM across multiple brands, ensuring data integrity, accurate segmentation, and clean contact and company records.

_Workflow & Automation: Design, build, and continuously optimize automated workflows for lead nurturing, deal progression, task assignments, and internal notifications.

_Pipeline Management: Set up and manage sales pipelines, deal stages, and custom properties aligned with each brand's unique sales process.

_Integrations: Configure and manage integrations between HubSpot and third-party tools (e.g., Slack, Zapier,
 platforms, and other MarTech solutions).

_Reporting & Dashboards: Build custom reports and dashboards to track KPIs across marketing, sales, and customer success and translate that data into actionable insights.


 Marketing: Create and manage
 campaigns, sequences, and templates that drive engagement and support funnel progression.

_User Training & Support: Onboard tea
mbers across brands to HubSpot and provide ongoing guidance on best practices and platform updates.

_Process Improvement: Regularly audit existing setups, identify inefficiencies, and implement improvements to keep operations running at peak performance.



Requirements

_3–5 years of hands-on HubSpot experience across Marketing Hub, Sales Hub, and/or Service Hub.

_Proven track record building workflows and automation that drive real business outcomes.

_Strong understanding of CRM best practices, including data hygiene, contact segmentation, and lifecycle stage management.

_Experience managing HubSpot integrations with third-party tools via native connectors or Zapier.

_Ability to build custom reports and dashboards that support strategic, data-driven decisions.

_HubSpot certifications (e.g., HubSpot Marketing Software, CRM, Sales Hub) are a strong plus.

_Solid understanding of sales and marketing funnels in a B2B or SaaS context.

_Strong analytical mindset with excellent problem-solving skills.

_Clear, confident communicator who can collaborate across multiple teams and brands simultaneously.



Nice to Have

_Experience with HubSpot Operations Hub (data sync, custom coded actions).

_Familiarity with HTML/CSS for
 and landing page customization.

_Background in digital marketing, sales operations, or revenue operations (RevOps).

_Prior experience working within an agency, incubator, or multi-brand environment.

What You’ll Do

Demonstrate exceptional communication skills with U.S. customers.

title: Global Company Operations Assistant (Inbox, Calendar, & Team Sync)
description:
About Us:

We are a growing auto body and digital marketing company based in Markham, Ontario, Canada. We operate a physical shop in Canada, coordinate with a support team in China, and manage remote technical staff in the Philippines. We need a highly organized Operations Assistant to act as our central hub—keeping the company's daily workflow synced, managing the main company inbox, and helping us screen new hires.



What You Will Do:



Team Follow-Up & Coordination: Act as the central communicator for the company. You will pass project instructions to our coworkers in China and the Philippines, and follow up daily to make sure everyone is completing their tasks on time so the Canadian shop keeps running smoothly.



Project Lookahead & Future Planning: Review the company's visible Google Calendar and active project lists. You will proactively look at what the company needs for the upcoming week and remind the global teams what assets or tasks they need to prepare in advance.



Company
 Management: Manage the main company
 inbox. You will read incoming messages, organize them, and prepare draft replies for customers and partners so our Canadian team can quickly review and hit "send."



Recruitment & Resume Screening: Help build our team by reading through incoming job applications for our technical AI roles. You will follow our strict checklist to filter out unqualified resumes and select only the top candidates for the company to interview.



Google Drive Organization: Act as the "librarian" for all company content. You will ensure that all global teams are uploading and downloading files, videos, and graphics from the correct folders without making a mess.



What We Are Looking For:



Proactive Communicator: You are comfortable politely messaging other tea
mbers to ask, "Has this task been finished yet? The Canadian office needs it today."



Forward-Thinking: You naturally look at next week's calendar to see what the company needs to prepare, rather than just waiting to be told what to do today.



Detail-Oriented: You can look at a resume and perfectly apply our "Yes/No" checklist to d
 if they are a good candidate for the company.



Time Zone Awareness: You deeply understand how to coordinate deadlines between Canada (EST), China (Beijing Time), and the Philippines (PHT).



Tech Proficiency: High-level comfort using Google Workspace (Drive, Calendar, Sheets, Gmail).

title: Head of Finance & Accounting
description:
Location: Remote (Philippines) – Must work U.S. hours



About Us



WA DeHart Inc., a U.S.-based wholesale distribution company headquartered in Pennsylvania, has been in business for over 100 years. With new leadership, capital, and technology, we are transforming into a tech-driven, growth-focused distribution platform serving convenience stores, gas stations, and smoke shops across the U.S.



Our industry is low-margin, high-volume, and heavily regulated, requiring precision, compliance, and operational discipline to succeed.



Role Overview



We are seeking an experienced Head of Finance & Accounting to oversee all accounting operations, compliance, and financial strategy for our growing organization.



This is a hands-on executive leadership role for a detail-oriented professional who can build scalable financial systems, maintain strict regulatory compliance, and partner with leadership on growth initiatives including cost optimization, capital raises, and acquisitions.



Key Responsibilities



Oversee all accounting operations across multiple entities, ensuring accuracy, transparency, and compliance



Develop and maintain strong financial controls, policies, and scalable accounting systems



Manage monthly, quarterly, and annual close processes, including preparation of financial statements and reports



Lead accounting, financial reporting, and compliance across U.S. operations, ensuring adherence to U.S. and Pennsylvania regulations; experience with FDA regulations, tobacco compliance, and PAC compliance is a strong asset



File required monthly reports with the Pennsylvania Department of Revenue and manage regulatory filings and audits



Partner with external auditors, CPAs, and accounting firms to produce reviewed or audited financial statements



Manage bank and lender relationships, including reporting requirements and loan covenant compliance



Support executive leadership with capital raises, investor reporting, and KPI monitoring



Lead budgeting, forecasting, cash flow management, and financial planning



Drive cost-cutting initiatives and operational efficiency, including vendor contract review, renegotiation of terms, and identifying savings opportunities



Represent the company in audits, tax reviews, and accounting disputes



Mentor and develop a small team of accountants/bookkeepers



Provide financial insights that support organic growth and potential acquisitions



Continuously improve the efficiency, scalability, and accuracy of financial operations



Perform other finance and accounting responsibilities as needed to support strategic, operational, and compliance objectives



Qualifications



10+ years of progressive experience in finance and accounting leadership roles



CPA, CA, or equivalent professional designation strongly preferred



Strong knowledge of financial reporting, regulatory compliance, and accounting controls



Experience managing vendor relationships, cost optimization initiatives, and financial efficiency projects



Experience working with banks, lenders, and financial institutions, including loan covenant compliance



Experience supporting capital raises, investor reporting, or financing activities



Strong business acumen with the ability to think like an owner



Highly detail-oriented, process-driven, and capable of building scalable financial systems



Experience leading and mentoring accounting teams



Preferred Experience



Experience managing multi-entity accounting environments



Experience in wholesale distribution, FMCG, or similar industries



What We Offer



Competitive compensation



Executive leadership role with direct impact on company growth



Opportunity to work closely with ownership and executive leadership



Career growth as the company expands into multiple locations



Long-term opportunities for growth within a scaling U.S. organization



Application Requirement



Please include a short voice or video recording briefly introducing yourself when submitting your application. Applications without a recording will not be considered.

title: Bilingual - Host Family Scheduler
description:
Apex Social brings together experienced educators, therapists and care providers with families who have special care needs and provides personal & career development through a unique, collaborative work experience for Care Professionals in a new state or new country.



Our Mission

-Unlocking children’s greatest potential through integrating skilled Care Professionals into their family’s daily life.

-Empowering Children to develop to their fullest abilities.

-Empowering Care Professionals by providing personal & career development, experiential learning and cultural enrichment through a unique, collaborative work experience.

-Empowering Families by providing specialized, skilled child care and cultural exchange in the home through an individualized recruiting and matching process.

-Empowering Schools & Universities by providing career coaching and support for their students and alumni to gain interdisciplinary paid work experience & continuing education in Germany, the United States and Australia.

-Empowering Tea
mbers by providing a collaborative, remote work environment with opportunities for training and growth as well as a commitment to our mission ensuring that we find purpose, fulfillment and a sense of belonging in our careers.



Position Overview

The Host Family Scheduler is responsible for coordinating and managing placement schedules between host families in the U.S. and participants, ensuring smooth communication and transitions across time zones. This role requires daily collaboration with U.S.-based teams and participants, while maintaining accurate records and schedules. The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, highly organized, and able to communicate effectively across cultures and time zones.



Key Responsibilities

-Manage scheduling and placement logistics between U.S. host families and participants, ensuring timely and accurate coordination.

-Maintain and update scheduling systems and records daily, reflecting current availability and assignments.

-Communicate proactively with host families, participants, and internal teams to confirm schedules, address questions, and provide updates.

-Collaborate with U.S.-based recruitment, placement, and operations teams to resolve scheduling conflicts and ensure smooth placements.

-Track and document all schedule changes, cancellations, or rescheduling requests promptly.

-Support onboarding and placement logistics, including arrival dates, orientations, and handoffs.

-Generate weekly reports on placement activity, scheduling updates, and outstanding tasks.

-Provide excellent customer service to host families and participants to ensure a positive experience.





Qualifications

Education: Associate’s or Bachelor’s degree preferred, or equivalent experience in scheduling, coordination, or administrative support.

Experience: Minimum 1–2 years of experience in scheduling, customer service, or administrative coordination (experience with international teams, education, exchange programs, or healthcare placement preferred).



Skills:

-Strong organizational and multitasking abilities.

-Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English and German.

-High attention to detail and accuracy.

-Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, Google Workspace, and scheduling software (e.g., Airtable, CRM systems), KEAP, Slack, and others.

-Ability to work independently and as part of a cross-cultural, remote team.

-Customer service–oriented mindset with strong problem-solving abilities.



Working Hours: 40 hours full time

Occasional flexibility may be required to accommodate urgent scheduling needs or communication with U.S.-based participants.



Compensation and Benefits

-Paid time off, holidays, and remote-friendly work environment.

-Opportunities for professional growth and cross-cultural experience with U.S.-based teams.

title: Tableau Developer (Snowflake)
description:
# Tableau Developer (Snowflake)



## Overview



We are looking for an experienced **BI Developer** to support a large-scale BI migration and ongoing analytics development. This role sits at the intersection of **data engineering, analytics engineering, and BI development**, with a focus on moving business logic out of Tableau and into governed Snowflake models.



You will work closely with BI developers, data engineers, and stakeholders to productionalize analytics logic, improve data sources, and maintain high-quality Tableau dashboards.



This role involves a mix of:



* Snowflake SQL development

* View creation and semantic layer design

* Git-based data development workflows

* Tableau dashboard development and maintenance

* Data validation and migration work



---



# Responsibilities



### Data Modeling & Snowflake Development



* Convert Tableau Custom SQL into governed Snowflake views

* Design and implement reporting-layer views and semantic models

* Optimize queries and Snowflake performance

* Maintain clear, reusable SQL models for analytics consumption

* Validate data accuracy across legacy and migrated sources



### Data Source Migration



* Assist with BI migrations and data source standardization

* Compare datasets using SQL to validate migration correctness

* Identify schema mismatches, missing fields, and logic differences

* Document data lineage and transformation logic



### Tableau Development



* Build and maintain Tableau dashboards

* Replace legacy data sources with optimized Snowflake views

* Refactor workbooks to remove embedded SQL logic

* Implement best practices for performance and maintainability



### Version Control & Deployment



* Manage SQL models and views through Git workflows

* Submit and review pull requests for data model changes

* Participate in CI/CD workflows for analytics code deployment

* Maintain documentation of data models and reporting logic



### Collaboration



* Work closely with BI developers, analytics engineers, and stakeholders

* Participate in technical design discussions for new data models

* Provide recommendations for improving data architecture

* Support ongoing analytics requests across business teams



---



# Required Skills



### Core Technical Skills



* Strong **SQL (Snowflake preferred)**

* Experience designing **analytics or reporting-layer views**

* Tableau development experience

* Git-based development workflows

* Experience validating datasets and debugging data discrepancies



### Data Engineering / Analytics Engineering



* Experience moving logic from BI tools into the warehouse

* Understanding of data modeling concepts (facts, dimensions, grain)

* Ability to optimize SQL for performance and maintainability

* Experience with data warehouse environments (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift)



### BI Development



* Tableau data source management

* Dashboard performance optimization

* Workbook refactoring and migration

* Understanding of Tableau extracts vs live connections



---



# Nice-to-Have Skills



* dbt experience

* Experience migrating BI systems or data sources

* Familiarity with marketing, finance, or product analytics datasets

* Experience building semantic layers or data marts

* Airflow or orchestration familiarity



---



# What Success Looks Like



Within the first 90 days, you will:



* Help complete ongoing BI data source migrations

* Productionalize several Snowflake views replacing Tableau Custom SQL

* Improve performance and maintainability of existing dashboards

* Establish repeatable patterns for analytics development in Snowflake



---



# Ideal Candidate



You are someone who:



* Is equally comfortable writing complex SQL and building Tableau dashboards

* Understands how BI tools interact with data warehouses

* Can translate messy analytics logic into clean, reusable data models

* Enjoys improving data systems and reducing technical debt

* Works independently and communicates clearly with both engineers and analysts



---



# Engagement Type



Flexible:



* Contract (preferred for migration support)

* Part-time to start, potential for full-time conversion



Fully Remote.

This position does not book loads.

The owner makes all final booking decisions.



???? Main Responsibilities



???? Load Searching (Training Provided)



You will:

•   Search for auto transport loads on load boards

•   Review pickup and delivery locations

•   Calculate rate per mile

•   Check total miles and route fit (I-10 strategy)

•   Send load options to the owner for approval

•   Keep load details organized in the tracking sheet



?? You will NOT:

•   Call brokers to book loads

•   Approve rates

•   Sign rate confirmations



Only the owner books loads.





???? Paperwork & Document Management



You will:

•   Send carrier packets (W9, insurance, MC, etc.)

•   Save signed broker agreements

•   Maintain organized digital folders

•   Keep insurance certificates updated

•   Track rate confirmations and BOLs



Organization is critical.





???? Accounting (REQUIRED)



You must have an accounting background and QuickBooks Online experience.



You will:

•   Create invoices in QuickBooks Online

•   Record factoring payments

•   Record all expenses (fuel, tolls, maintenance, etc.)

•   Upload and attach receipt photos

•   Categorize all transactions correctly

•   Match bank deposits

•   Reconcile accounts monthly

•   Generate weekly Profit & Loss reports



?? No uncategorized transactions allowed.

?? Clean books are mandatory.





???? Factoring Submission



You will:

•   Submit signed BOL and rate confirmation to factoring company

•   Verify paperwork is complete

•   Track payment status

•   Follow up if payments are delayed

•   Record factoring fees properly in QuickBooks



Cash flow must stay clean and organized.





???? Weekly Reporting



Every week you will provide:

•   Total revenue

•   Total expenses

•   Fuel cost summary

•   Net profit

•   Outstanding invoices

•   Any financial issues





This supports the San Man operating model.



???? Required Qualifications

•   Accounting background (required)

•   QuickBooks Online experience (required)

•   Strong attention to detail

•   Organized and structured

•   Comfortable using load boards

•   Basic math skills (rate per mile calculation)

•   Reliable internet connection

•   Ability to work independently, Limited phone calls



Dispatch experience is NOT required.

We will train you on load searching.



???? Important Rules

•   Do not book loads.

•   Do not approve rates.

•   Do not leave paperwork incomplete.

•   Do not delay invoices.

•   No missing receipts.

•   No uncategorized QuickBooks entries.

•   Report problems immediately.



???? Performance Will Be Measured By

•   Accuracy in QuickBooks

•   Organization of documents

•   Speed of invoice creation

•   Clean factoring submissions

•   Quality of weekly reports

•   Consistency and reliability



???? This Position Is NOT For

•   Someone careless with numbers

•   Someone uncomfortable using QuickBooks online or Trucking office software.

•   Someone who ignores details

•   Someone who needs constant supervision

•   Someone who avoids responsibility



I would like all responses to this inquiry to be provided in a short video format.

This role is the financial and paperwork control center of the company.

title: Sourcing & Procurement Specialist – Manufacturing Parts
description:
Company: Top End Sports LLC  

Website: 
  



About Us  

Top End Sports LLC designs and manufactures high-performance sports wheelchairs and adaptive athletic equipment used by athletes around the world. We are a growing manufacturing company focused on quality, performance, and innovation.



Position Overview  

We are seeking a experienced, highly organized and resourceful Sourcing & Procurement Specialist to help identify, qualify, and manage suppliers for components used in our products. This role will focus on sourcing machined parts, accessories, hardware, and fasteners from domestic and international vendors while maintaining quality, cost efficiency, and reliable lead times.



Responsibilities



- Source and identify manufacturers and suppliers for machined aluminum parts, hardware, accessories, and fasteners  

- Request and evaluate supplier quotes based on technical drawings and specifications  

- Work with machine shops and component vendors to obtain prototypes and production pricing  

- Develop relationships with domestic and international suppliers  

- Negotiate pricing, lead times, and minimum order quantities  

- Track supplier performance including cost, quality, and delivery  

- Coordinate with engineering and production teams to ensure parts meet specifications  

- Identify cost reduction opportunities and alternate suppliers  

- Assist with managing purchase orders and supplier communications



Qualifications



- Experience in sourcing or procurement for manufacturing  

- Familiarity with machined components, CNC parts, and hardware sourcing  

- Ability to interpret technical drawings or CAD files (SolidWorks experience is a plus)  

- Strong negotiation and supplier communication skills  

- Experience working with international suppliers is preferred  

- Highly organized and detail-oriented



Preferred Experience



- Manufacturing or product development environment  

- Experience sourcing parts in industries such as sports equipment, bicycles, medical devices, or mechanical assemblies  

- Knowledge of aluminum machining, anodizing, and specialty hardware



Compensation



Compensation will be based on experience.



Kindly highlight your experience sourcing manufacturing components when applying for this position.

title: Job Title: Virtual Assistant – Property Research, Quoting & Admin Support
description:
About the Role:

We are looking for a highly organized, detail-oriented, and proactive Virtual Assistant to join our team and support the daily operations of our land surveying business. Your primary focus will be assisting with opening new jobs and conducting detailed research on property records in Ontario, Canada. As a secondary focus, you will help prepare and track client quotes, alongside handling general administrative tasks.



If you excel at digging into databases, ensuring data accuracy, and managing workflows without letting details fall through the cracks, we want to hear from you. This is strictly an operational support role, not a sales or business development position.



Key Responsibilities:



Job Initiation & Property Research: Assist in opening new jobs by researching property records, land registries, parcel data, and historical survey information for locations across Ontario, Canada.



Quote Preparation & Tracking: Draft, send, and follow up on client quotes or estimates. You will manually track the status of all quotes (Pending, Accepted, Rejected) to ensure our pipeline stays organized until this process is automated.



Data Management: Build and maintain accurate records, databases, and project files with the information gathered during property research.



Administrative Support: Handle
 correspondence, manage calendars, organize digital files, and assist with basic documentation to keep operations running smoothly.



Requirements:



Excellent written and verbal English communication skills.



Strong internet research skills, specifically with the ability to navigate complex databases, maps, or municipal websites (prior experience with real estate, legal, or property research is a huge plus).



Exceptional attention to detail—you must be the type of person who double-checks addresses, lot numbers, and client names before finalizing a document.



Strong organizational skills with experience tracking workflows, quotes, or pipelines.



High proficiency in Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Drive) or Microsoft Office.



A reliable, high-speed internet connection and a working computer.



Ability to work full-time hours, specifically 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM Eastern Standard Time (EST).



Contract Details & Schedule:



Role Type: Independent Contractor



Schedule: Full-time, 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM EST (Monday to Friday).



Compensation: To be discussed based on experience.



How to Apply:

IMPORTANT: We are looking for someone who pays close attention to detail. Do not send a generic cover letter. To be considered for an interview, you must answer the following questions in your application message:



What is your experience with online research, specifically navigating government databases, property records, or mapping tools?



If I asked you to find property details and historical data for a specific address in Ontario, Canada, what initial steps would you take to find accurate information online?



We currently track quotes manually. How would you set up a system (using spreadsheets or free tools) to track quotes sent to clients and ensure we follow up with them on time?



What tools do you prefer to use for organizing data and managing your administrative tasks?



Are you available to work 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM Eastern Standard Time (EST)? Please confirm.



To show you have read this entire post, please make the subject line of your application: "Ontario Surveyor VA - [Your Favorite Color]"

title: 3D Blender Artist
description:
We’re looking for a highly skilled 3D modeling expert in Blender to help us create a premium, beautiful, modern interface for an upcoming audio plugin.



You’ll be working from concept sketches and references to build a polished 3D plugin GUI that feels high-end, clean, and visually striking. This role is ideal for someone who understands not just modeling, but also how to make digital products feel premium through materials, reflections, lighting, depth, and presentation.



This project is for Vox Samples, a fast-growing Australia-based audio software brand focused on helping music producers work faster with intuitive, professional tools. Vox Samples focuses on speed, intuitive controls, ready-to-use presets. With 150,000+ active users and 1,300+ 5-star reviews, Vox Samples build products to help producers get results faster.



You’ll have the chance to work directly with the Founder & CEO, contribute to a real commercial product, and help shape the visual identity of a plugin used by music producers worldwide.



What you’ll do:

1. Create a high-quality 3D model of an audio plugin interface in Blender

2. Translate supplied sketches into a production-ready visual design

3. Build clean, premium surfaces with accurate materials such as metal, glass, plastic, glow, and textured finishes

4. Set up lighting and rendering that makes the product feel modern and professional

5. Match the style of premium plugin interfaces used in the music software industry

6. Deliver the editable Blender source file along with rendered assets as needed

7. Iterate based on feedback from the Founder/CEO



What we’re looking for:

1. Strong experience in Blender

2. Excellent eye for form, proportion, materials, lighting, and detail

3. Ability to create clean, realistic, premium-looking product renders

4. Experience designing UI/product visuals, ideally for software, hardware, or audio/music tools

5. Ability to work from rough sketches and turn them into polished final visuals

6. Good communication and willingness to revise based on feedback

7. Must provide the original Blender file upon project completion



Bonus points if you have

1. Experience creating visuals for audio plugins, music software, hardware, or tech products

2. A portfolio showing premium product rendering or interface-related 3D work

3. Understanding of what makes plugin interfaces appealing to music producers

4. Experience with glow effects, brushed metal materials, glass, LED meters, and high-end product presentation



Why join us:

1. Work directly with the Founder & CEO

2. Help shape a product for a growing audio software company

3. Opportunity for ongoing work on future plugin visuals and product designs

4. Be part of a brand serving a large and growing audience of music producers worldwide

title: Video Editor
description:
About Us



We are looking for a full-time Video Editor for our YouTube channels:

• 

• 



We make documentary-style videos that explain complex stories in a clear and engaging way.



About the Role



You should enjoy editing and crafting strong visual stories. You must be proficient in Adobe Premiere Pro and comfortable using Adobe After Effects when needed. You should also be open to learning Geolayer3 (an After Effects plugin for map animations).



This is a full-time, remote role, Monday to Friday, 9 AM to 5 PM Cyprus time.



We typically produce one to two 20-minute videos per week.





Workflow

1. You receive the script.

2. Assistant Editors gather source materials (photos, videos, AI assets, etc.) and organize them in Dropbox.

3. You begin editing using Premiere Pro and AE when required.

4. A Generalist Animator handles all 3D and 2D animations, but you should understand how they fit into the timeline.

5. When you finish your edit, you send it to Senior Editors for review.

6. Senior Editors make adjustments and prepare it for final approval.

7. After approval, the video moves to Colour Grading and Sound Design

8. Final management approval.

9. Export and delivery.



Qualifications



Please send:

• Your CV

• A showreel or 2–3 videos that best represent your work



Required Skills

- Strong proficiency in Adobe Premiere Pro

- Basic to intermediate skills in Adobe After Effects

- Willingness to learn Geolayer3 map animations



Preferred Skills

- Experience with 2D or 3D animations

- Familiarity with organized Dropbox workflows.

Are you obsessed with AI tools? Do you know how to write the perfect prompts in ChatGPT and Gemini to generate high-quality text and images? Are you a Canva wizard who loves putting together scroll-stopping social media creatives?



If yes, we want you on our team!

Semi-Automation Setup: Develop workflows and detailed prompts using ChatGPT/Gemini to semi-automate the generation of our social media creatives, making our process faster and more efficient.



Team Creative Support: Act as the primary "Creative Support" for the entire team. Whenever the team needs graphics, banners, or visual assets, you are the go-to person!



Client Reporting: Assist the team in pulling data and creating/formatting monthly progress reports for our clients.



Requirements & Qualifications:



Strong, proven experience using ChatGPT and Gemini (specifically for marketing, copywriting, and image generation).



Excellent proficiency in Canva.



A strong understanding of "Prompt Engineering" – you know exactly what to tell the AI to get the specific output you need.



A good eye for design, layout, and social media aesthetics.



Excellent English communication and writing skills.



Highly organized, detail-oriented, and able to handle multiple creative requests from the team.



Salary & Compensation:

title: Global AI Ops & Social Media Manager + Bonus
description:
About the Company & Role:

We are a highly successful auto body and automotive service business based in Markham, Ontario, Canada. We operate internationally, coordinating with a technical/support team in China.



We are hiring our "First-in-Command" AI Operations & Social Media Manager based in the Philippines. You will build and oversee our entire outbound marketing and lead generation machine across all major platforms:
, Instagram, TikTok, Xiaohongshu (Little Red Book), and more. You will build AI pipelines, manage day-to-day operations, and eventually hire and lead a team of Virtual Assistants under you.



Compensation & Bonus Structure:



Base Pay: $5.50 to $7.00 USD per hour (Depending on experience and technical skills).



Performance Bonus: You will receive a $10 USD bonus for every single client your team generates who actually pays for our auto body services. There is no cap on this bonus!



Your Daily Responsibilities:



Multi-Platform Lead Generation: Oversee the daily lead generation strategy across Meta (FB/IG), TikTok, and Xiaohongshu. Ensure safe login practices (VPNs/VPS) to prevent account bans.



Global Coordination: Act as the central operational bridge. You will work closely with our Canada team (EST) for sales/booking and our China team (Beijing time) to coordinate culturally accurate content for platforms like Xiaohongshu.



AI Tool Research & Pipeline Building: Continuously hunt for new AI tools. Build automated pipelines (Zapier, 
, ChatGPT API) to streamline content posting and pull lead data from social media directly into our systems.



Daily Operations Management: Manage the daily shop schedule using Google Calendar (booking client estimates) and organize all company assets, vehicle photos, reports, and SOPs in Google Drive.



Team Building: As we scale, you will interview, hire, and manage 2-3 entry-level Setters to handle the manual DM outreach across the different platforms.



Requirements:



Exceptional English communication skills (written and spoken)—you must be a strong closer in chat.



Proven ability to build automations/pipelines (Zapier, Make, etc.) and integrate new AI tools.



Deep knowledge of social media safety limits (shadowbans, IP tracking).



Highly organized with Google Workspace (Drive, Calendar, Sheets).



(Bonus) Experience working with international teams or an understanding of Asian social media platforms is a huge plus!

• Other small tasks that help the team save time



Some days the tasks will be very simple. Other days you may help with research or organization. Flexibility is important.



What we are looking for:



• Organized and detail-oriented

• Comfortable doing repetitive tasks

• Able to follow instructions carefully

• Reliable and responsive

• Comfortable reminding the team about pending tasks

• Good written English

• Comfortable working with new or evolving tasks that may not always have perfect instructions

Important



We are looking for someone who is proactive and responsible. If you notice something that needs follow-up or a task that has been forgotten, we appreciate someone who can remind us and help keep things moving.



Application Instructions



To apply, please send a short Loom video (2–3 minutes) to:





In your Loom video, please:



Introduce yourself



Tell us about your previous VA experience



Tell us about the most unusual or crazy task you’ve ever had to do as an assistant.



At the beginning of your video, please say:

“I am ready to help the team stay organized.”



This helps us know you carefully read the instructions.

title: AI Automation & Lead Generation Assistant
description:

title: Operations Virtual Assistant
description:
Note: Interested applicants must submit their application exclusively through the OnlineJobs platform. Do not
 the company directly. Any candidate who contacts the company outside of the OnlineJobs system will be disqualified from consideration.



Operations Virtual Assistant

(Process-Driven | High-Precision | Behind-the-Scenes Operations)



Job Overview



Maid In America Cleaning Services is hiring an Operations Virtual Assistant to protect schedule integrity, data accuracy, and operational consistency.



This is a behind-the-scenes execution role. You will not be customer-facing. You will work inside defined systems and SOPs to ensure our operational engine runs cleanly and efficiently every day.



If you enjoy structured work, moving quickly without sacrificing accuracy, and taking pride in clean execution, this role will feel natural to you.



What You’ll Do:



1. Scheduling & Job Management (Primary Responsibility)

You will own scheduling preparation and system accuracy for our TN locations.



This includes:

- Creating and modifying clients, jobs, and visits in Jobber

- Preparing future-day schedules for review and approval

- Creating training tasks in Jobber for new tea
mbers

- Re-sequencing jobs and rebalancing teams within defined guardrails

- Maintaining week-to-week schedule consistency whenever possible

- Flagging and correcting conflicts or inefficiencies early

- Executing same-day scheduling adjustments exactly as directed



2. Data Accuracy & System Integrity

You will ensure operational data is accurate, complete, and reliable.



This includes:

- Updating customer notes, job frequencies, and service details

- Invoicing customers promptly after service completion

- Calculating cost-of-service data after jobs

- Preparing timesheet and tips reports for payroll

- Logging complaints and rectified cleans accurately

- Creating and publishing weekly operational reports

- Manual KPI data entry

- Verifying work-to-labor ratios using company tools



3. Administrative Support

You will support operational structure and remove administrative friction.



This includes:

- Supporting the Assistant Operations Manager with administrative execution

- Maintaining internal trackers and shared documents

- Following established documentation and checklists consistently



What This Role Requires

- High pace with high accuracy

- Comfort with structured, repeatable tasks

- Strong attention to detail

- Clear communication when issues arise

- Strong adherence defined systems and guardrails

- Ability to work independently within clear expectations



This role involves a high volume of operational tasks that must be completed correctly and consistently. As the company grows, volume will increase — and this role grows with it.



Who We’re Looking For



Detail-Oriented & Reliable

You take pride in doing things correctly and thoroughly.



High Throughput Executor

You can handle volume without losing structure or focus.



Process-Driven

You work well inside defined systems and appreciate clear expectations.



Accountable Communicator

You flag issues early, own mistakes quickly, and ask for clarification when needed.



Tech-Comfortable

You’re comfortable using tools like Slack, Google Suite, Chrome extensions, and scheduling platforms. Experience with Jobber is a strong plus.



Experienced in Fast-Paced Admin or Scheduling

Background in administrative, dispatch, logistics, scheduling, or operations-heavy roles is

preferred.



Work Requirements

- Monday–Friday, 8AM–5PM U.S. Central Time

- Reliable internet with backup

- Dedicated workspace

- Backup power options in case of outages

- Fluent written English



Our Core Values

- Communication

- Connection

- Quality

- Service

- Teamwork



If these values resonate with you, we want to hear from you.



Important Screening Step (Read Carefully)



SERIOUS APPLICANTS ONLY:

1. Complete our Culture Index survey: 

2. Submit your application with the subject line beginning with the core value you most resonate with (ALL CAPS).

3. Use a minimum of 5 Apply Points when applying

4. In your application message, include one sentence describing the most repetitive task you have performed at a high standard for 6+ months.



Applications that do not follow all instructions will not be reviewed.

mbers who help the company grow.
