title: Full-time Google Ads role GA4/GTM Tracking Experience
description:
Full-Time | $7.50/hour | Weekdays | UK Timezone (9am–5pm)



I’m hiring a Google Ads Campaign Builder who lives for clean builds: tight structure, correct settings, strong conversion tracking, and launch-ready accounts. Most of our work comes from Fiverr setup projects, so campaign setup is the core deliverable. Ongoing management/optimisation exists, but it’s secondary.



Work hours: Mon–Fri, 9:00–5:00 UK time



**The mission**



Ship high-quality Google Ads builds and tracking setups fast, with zero sloppy tracking and clean handoffs so accounts perform from day one.



**Your core responsibilities (80% setup)**

1) Google Ads campaign builds (primary)



Build and launch campaigns (Search, PMax; Display/Remarketing when needed)



Keyword research and campaign architecture (tight themes, clear intent mapping)



Ad groups, ads, assets/extensions, audiences, locations, schedules



Correct bidding + budget setup based on goal (leads/eCom) and conversion signal readiness



Pre-launch QA checklist (settings, brand exclusions where needed, geo intent, networks, conversions, URLs, tracking templates)



2) Tracking implementation & validation (primary)



Set up and troubleshoot GTM + GA4



Configure conversion tracking (forms, calls, purchases, lead events)



Validate conversions end-to-end (test submissions, deduping, attribution sanity checks)



Fix common issues: missing params, duplicates, incorrect event firing, consent mode issues where relevant



**Secondary responsibilities (20% management)**



Light optimisation after launch (search terms + negatives, basic asset improvements, quick wins)



Performance notes for handoff / next steps



Support troubleshooting on active accounts as needed



**Requirements**



You’ve built many Google Ads accounts from scratch (not just “managed”)



Strong GA4 + GTM setup/debugging



You can produce clean, documented builds that another buyer can pick up



You work fast without sacrificing QA



You communicate blockers early



Nice to have: Shopify/Woo tracking, CallRail, Looker Studio, Meta Ads



**How to apply (mandatory)**



30-sec intro video: who you are + what you build best

title: Appointment Setter (Experienced B2B Cold Caller/Telemarketer)
description:
* PLEASE READ THE ENTIRE DESCRIPTION BEFORE APPLYING *





IMPORTANT:



- We are ONLY looking for candidates with previous B2B cold calling experience.

- If you have only worked warm leads, inbound calls, or appointment setting from ads, do NOT apply.



COMPANY INFO:



- My name is Jordan. I am the founder of a fast-growing company that helps small businesses never miss a call using an A.I. Receptionist.

- This is a simple, high-demand service that business owners immediately understand. We are looking for 1–2 strong cold callers to help introduce it to small business owners across the U.S.

- We are not a large corporate call center. We are a small team with an owner focussed on helping the team get real results.



WHAT YOU WILL DO:



- Source your own B2B leads

- Cold call small business owners

- Book them for a free consultation

- Ensure they show up to the call

- That's it! You do not close deals. Our closing team handles everything, your only job is to get them to show-up.



MUST HAVE (DO NOT APPLY IF THIS DOES NOT DESCRIBE YOU):



- Proven B2B cold calling experience

- Strong English with no heavy accent

- Reliable internet and a quiet workspace

- Comfortable making high-volume outbound calls

- Put the phrase “Dial And Smile” in your subject line so we know you read this



COMPENSATION:



-$2/hour base

-$25 per attended show-up

- $100 per closed deal

- Monthly performance bonuses



HOURS:



- Part-time (20 hours per week) with the option to transition to full time

- Schedule: 2:00 AM – 6:00 AM Philippine Time



HOW TO APPLY:



- Do not send a generic message.

- In your application, include 2 things: Your specific B2B cold calling experience AND why this role fits you

- Do not use chat GPT or similar responses, those will be rejected. We read each application and looking for a genuine person to reach out.

title: Ecommerce UGC Ads Video Editor (TikTok / Reels / Shorts) -Dropshipping
description:
Full application here







1. PAY

* $2 per approved video

* $50 bonus per winning/validated ad

* $150/month retainer (first month, 80 ads)

* Retainer increases after proving performance



2. JOB TYPE

* Part-time to full-time

* Long-term

* Remote

* US-based ecommerce brand [using a high-volume testing model (CaniCol v2.2)]



3. WHAT YOU’LL DO

* Edit short-form vertical ads (15–35 sec) for: TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts

* Create 80–100 ads per month

 * Work only with existing assets: Product clips, b-roll, images, Competitor ads, Reviews & screenshots, AI-generated or AI-assisted clips

* Create multiple versions using: Different hooks (first 1–3 seconds), Different pacing, angles, and CTAs

* Add: Clear subtitles, On-screen text, Benefits, social proof, urgency

* Follow file naming and Google Drive structure

* Make fast revisions based on ad performance



4. REQUIREMENTS

* Experience editing short-form vertical videos

* Can use CapCut, Premiere Pro, Final Cut, or DaVinci Resolve

* Understand hooks and fast pacing

* Comfortable working with existing footage only

* Can create clean English subtitles

* Reliable PC/laptop + stable internet



5. IDEAL TRAITS

* Detail-oriented

* Follows instructions well

* Can hit weekly output targets

* Open to feedback

* Looking for a long-term role, not a one-time gig



6. SCHEDULE & TOOLS

* Flexible schedule

* Must overlap 2–3 hours with US Eastern Time

* Tools used: Slack, Google Drive, 
, ClickUp, and Notion



7. HOW TO APPLY:

Use the exact subject line BLUE – Short-Form Ads Video Editor when applying. In your application, start with a short introduction of 3–5 sentences explaining who you are, your experience with short-form video ads, and why this high-volume editing role is a good fit for you. Include links to 3–6 samples of your vertical short-form ads (TikTok, Reels, or Shorts).



In the same message, clearly answer the following questions in plain text: what editing software you use daily, how many short-form ads you can realistically edit in one full working day, whether you have experience with ecommerce or dropshipping brands, whether you have used TikTok or Meta Ad Library to study or repurpose ads, and if you are comfortable with a compensation structure of $150 per month plus $50 per validated product.



Lastly, include your computer specifications (CPU, RAM, and GPU if applicable) and your internet speed. As an optional but strong plus, you may also share a 1–2 minute Loom or unlisted YouTube video where you explain one ad you edited and how you built the hook, middle, and call to action.



8. HIRING PROCESS

* Review applications (instructions must be followed)

* Shortlist based on portfolio & answers

* Paid test task

* 1-month paid trial

* Long-term role if successful

title: Product Researcher & Lister & Store Builder - Full-Time Position
description:
Full application here







Remote Ecom Operator (Dropshipping) — Run the Full Playbook End-to-End



SOPS please read before hand.















## Remote Ecom Operator (Dropshipping) — Run the Full Playbook End-to-End



We’re hiring a **hands-on, end-to-end dropshipping operator** to execute our complete operating system from **niche selection product research  supplier sourcing  store build  ads testing  fulfillment. This is not a “VA” role—this is an **owner-operator** style execution seat using a defined SOP playbook. 



### What you’ll be responsible for (end-to-end)



#### 1) Niche selection & market validation



* Use a structured niche scoring + validation process (Reddit + Google Trends + Amazon + TikTok signals) to pick testable niches. 



#### 2) Product research & competitor intelligence



* Run winning-product research using spy tools + checklists (e.g., Minea/PiPiADS + validation criteria). 

* Build product test shortlists, document competitors, extract angles/hooks, and recommend go/no-go decisions.



#### 3) Supplier sourcing, vetting & backup suppliers



* Source suppliers (CJ/AutoDS/Zendrop/Spocket/AliExpress/Alibaba as needed), vet with clear standards, and maintain **at least 2 suppliers per winning product**. 

* Confirm shipping methods/timelines and ensure policy-safe claims + realistic customer expectations.



#### 4) Shopify store build & conversion optimization



* Build/optimize Shopify store(s): theme setup, required apps, product pages, pricing, trust elements, FAQs, upsells. 

* Maintain mobile-first performance and clean customer journey (product page ? checkout ? post-purchase).



#### 5) Paid ads launch, testing & optimization (TikTok + Meta)



* Launch tests using structured testing protocols (budgeting, ad group structure, creative requirements, kill/scale rules). 

* Install pixels Day 1, monitor key metrics daily, iterate creatives fast, and pivot when data says so. 



---



## Systems & deliverables (how we work)



You will document and manage execution so work is “handoff-ready” and repeatable:



* Maintain product records, tasks, and structured research/decision packets (R0R3 style gates are a plus). 

* Keep organized folders and clear handoffs for creatives, store assets, suppliers, and performance reporting.



---



## Required experience



* Proven track record running **Shopify dropshipping or DTC testing** end-to-end (not just one slice like “product research” or “customer support”).

* Strong paid ads fundamentals: TikTok Ads + Meta Ads (testing structure, reading metrics, creative iteration, scaling decisions). 

* Comfortable owning operations: supplier coordination, fulfillment workflows, support processes, and refund/chargeback prevention. 





### Tools you are required to have and know how to use (At least 2)



Minea, PiPiADS, Meta Ad Library, Kalodata, Winning Hunter 

---



## What success looks like (core KPIs)



You will be measured on:



* Speed to launch: consistent product testing cadence (weekly/biweekly depending on scope).

* Store health: conversion rate trends, AOV improvements via upsells, refund rate control. 



---



## Role details



* **Remote / online**

* **Type:** Contract (with potential to grow into long-term operator role)

* **Hours:** Full-time 35–45 hrs/week]



  * Hourly: $1 to start

  * Performance bonus: $200 per winning product and funnel tied to net profit milestones (preferred for senior operators)



---



## How to apply



Send:



1. A short summary of your relevant wins (store results, ad spend levels, ROAS ranges, niches).

2. Links or screenshots: ad dashboards, store examples, creative examples



### Screening questions (answer in your application)



* What’s your step-by-step process to find and validate a product before spending on ads?

* Walk us through how you structure a $50/day TikTok test and what metrics d
 “kill” vs “keep.” 

* What’s your approach to reducing refunds/chargebacks in dropshipping? 

* What core
 flows do you launch first and why? 

* What tools from this stack have you personally used? 

* Full Understanding to the inital Compensation of 1USD base and 200 USD Per Validated Store/Product

* A fun fact about yourself so we know this was not written with AI

title: Luxury Shopify Store Builder for Branded Wellness E-Commerce (DriveOn)
description:

Long-Term Project | $50USD paid upon completion of each store | Filipino Applicants Only



ABOUT THE BRAND



DriveOn is a high-end wellness and performance brand focused on:



Recovery equipment



Mobility tools



Fitness and home gym accessories



Golf performance and biomechanics



Premium protocols and systems



Our standard is simple:

We operate like a luxury DTC brand, not a typical dropshipping store.

Everything must look and feel at the same level as Therabody, Hyperice, and Peloton.



We are building a full-scale Shopify website that reflects this brand identity and presents DriveOn as a premium authority in wellness and athletic performance.



ROLE OVERVIEW



We are hiring one highly skilled Shopify Store Builder who can execute a complete, premium, branded e-commerce build.



This role requires:



Strong Shopify implementation skills



Clear understanding of branded niche stores (not general stores)



Ability to follow a detailed specification document



High attention to detail



Professional, polished UI/UX standards



Strong communication and daily reporting



This is a project-based role with the potential for long-term work.



COMPENSATION

Documentation

REQUIRED DOCUMENT REVIEW (MUST READ BEFORE APPLYING)

DriveOn Shopify Build Specification (Google Doc):





Applicants who do NOT reference something specific from this document will not be considered.



WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT

Not a simple template installation



Not a beginner project



Not for applicants who cannot follow strict branding rules

DAILY COMMUNICATION REQUIREMENT

title: Shopify and Landing Page Builder for Dropshipping
description:
Full application here







About the role:

We’re looking for a landing page builder to help us quickly create and optimize conversion-focused pages for new e-commerce products.

We run a high-volume product testing model, typically testing 40–50 products. That means you’ll have many chances for “wins” and the associated $100 validation bonuses when your pages perform.

If you’re strong in Shopify/landing page builders, basic UX, and persuasive layout, this is a great opportunity for steady weekly income plus upside.



SOP







Compensation:



$10 USD per landing page built and approved (fixed weekly payment)



$100 USD bonus for every store that validates (details below)



Historically
a month retainer based on past validation numbers







Responsibilities:



Build simple, high-converting landing pages for single-product or small catalog e-commerce “stores”



Use our product briefs, angles, and references to structure pages that sell



Handle layout and structure (hero, benefits, social proof, FAQ, CTA sections, etc.)



Implement pages using no-code tools (for example: Shopify page builders like GemPages, PageFly, Shogun, or Webflow/other similar tools)



Collaborate with our team on iterations once we see early data



Ensure pages are mobile-first, fast-loading, and clean

Note: You will not be responsible for running ads or managing traffic. Your focus is building and refining landing pages/stores.

What “validation” means (how you earn the $100 bonus):



A store/landing page is considered validated when it hits both of the following during our testing window (usually the first 7–14 days after launch with paid traffic):



Sales threshold:



At least $1000 in tracked sales from cold traffic (excluding refunds and test orders)



Customer threshold:



At least 20 unique paying customers

When a store that you built meets these criteria during one of our test cycles, you earn a $100 USD bonus for that store, paid on top of your weekly $50.



Because we test 40–50 products, and you’ll be building for multiple products over time, there is a real opportunity to stack bonuses as more stores validate.



What we provide:



Clear product briefs (features, benefits, audience)



Reference sites and examples when available



Guidance on brand voice and style



A consistent pipeline of products to work on



Fast feedback based on performance data (what’s working / what isn’t)

Requirements (must-haves):



Proven experience building conversion-focused landing pages or e-commerce storefronts



Familiarity with at least one landing page or store builder (Shopify + GemPages/PageFly/Shogun, Webflow, or similar)



Strong grasp of layout, hierarchy, and clarity (you understand what makes a page easy to scan and buy from)



Ability to work from a brief and move quickly with minimal hand-holding



Reliable communication and consistent weekly delivery

Nice-to-haves:



Experience with DTC brands, dropshipping, or product testing environments



Basic understanding of CRO (A/B tests, above-the-fold design, scarcity, social proof)



Light copy-editing skills (comfortable polishing headlines and section text)

Time commitment and workflow:



Ongoing role with weekly work



Expect a few landing pages/stores per week, depending on product volume and your speed



We will start with a short test task (one landing page) to ensure mutual fit

Compensation summary:



Base: $50/week guaranteed, paid through Upwork



Bonus: $100 for each validated store you build (as defined above)



Upside: With 40–50 products in our testing pipeline, strong builders have many chances to hit multiple validations and bonuses over time

How to apply:

Please apply via these qustions and include:



A short introduction (1–2 paragraphs) about your landing page/e-commerce experience



3–5 examples of landing pages or stores you’ve built (links/portfolio)



What tools/page builders you are most comfortable with



Your approximate weekly availability (in hours) and your time zone



Compensation confirmation: Our structure is $50 USD per week base pay, plus a $100 USD bonus for every store you build that validates. Do you fully understand and agree to this compensation model?



Portfolio: Please share 3–5 links to landing pages or e-commerce stores you have personally built, and briefly describe your role in each.



Tools and stack: Which platforms and page builders are you most experienced with plus use of shrine (e.g., Shopify, GemPages, PageFly, Shogun, Webflow, etc.)? Please list them and indicate your proficiency level for each



Availability and speed: How many hours per week can you reliably commit, and how many simple product landing pages can you usually complete in a week?



Experience with testing and performance: Have you worked in a product testing or high-iteration environment before (e.g., testing many products or variations quickly)? If yes, describe your experience and how you used performance data to improve pages.



Deliverables

Build and optimize Shopify store

Design high-converting landing pages

Integrate payment gateways

Ensure mobile responsiveness

Optimize for SEO



If you do not answer these questions your application will not be considered.

title: Creative Ad Development, CRO and Media Buying Specialist Meta and Google
description:
Read This First





We are not looking for an ad button-pusher.



We are looking for one operator who can take full ownership of:

• Customer angles

• Creative direction

• Copywriting

• Competitor ad analysis

• Campaign setup, launch, and iteration



This starts as a paid trial using an existing store. dayflow.shop on a deliverable basis.

If you perform, this becomes long-term ownership across multiple brands with a 4 percent profit share ongoing. Performance upside is huge.



If you need:

• Hand-holding

• Preset SOPs

• Someone else to “approve everything”



Do not apply.



?



Context



We operate a branded wellness ecommerce store called Dayflow.

• 15–20 generic but validated wellness products

(massagers, eye masks, recovery tools, comfort products)

• Store is functional:

• Pages are acceptable

• Funnels work

• Traffic flows correctly



What’s missing:

• Sharp customer angles

• Strong creative leadership

• Cohesive messaging

• A single owner accountable for performance



This role exists to fix that gap.



?



Your Responsibility (End-to-End Ownership)



1. Full Store & Funnel Audit



You will independently:

• Audit the store end-to-end

• Identify:

• Messaging gaps

• Angle mismatches

• Product prioritization mistakes

• Conversion friction

• D
:

• What to keep

• What to change

• What to ignore



You will implement changes, not just suggest them.



?



2. Customer Angles & Positioning



You must:

• Define real buyer avatars

• Map:

• Pain ? mechanism ? outcome

• Assign specific angles to specific products

• Kill weak products or angles if needed



Generic “busy professionals / self-care” thinking will be rejected.



?



3. Creative Direction + Production



You fully own creative execution:

• Find and analyze competitor ads (Meta / TikTok)

• Either:

• Edit creatives yourself

• OR create clear creative briefs and hooks that convert

• D
:

• Hooks

• Formats

• Proof mechanisms

• CTA style



You may also 1 to 1 swipe the ads for testing



You do not need to film UGC,

but you must know how to direct it precisely.



?



4. Copywriting (Required)



You will write:

• Ad copy

• Primary text

• Headlines

• Angle-specific messaging



If you outsource copy in your process, do not apply.

We need someone who understands copy as part of the system, not a handoff.



?



5. Paid Ads Execution



You will:

• Set up clean testing structures

• Launch campaigns (Meta primary; TikTok optional bonus)

• Iterate based on signal

• Kill losers decisively

• Communicate performance clearly



This is operator logic, not dashboard theater.



?



What We’re Looking For (Non-Negotiable)

• Proven experience running DTC / dropshipping ads

• Strong creative intuition + analytical judgment

• Comfortable making decisions without approval loops

• Able to own results, not just tasks

• Clear communicator who can explain why something works or fails



?



What This Is NOT

• ? Not a beginner role

• ? Not “just media buying”

• ? Not a freelancer who waits for instructions

• ? Not someone who avoids accountability



If you’ve never been the final decision-maker on a store, this is not for you.



?



Trial ? Long-Term Path



This engagement begins as a paid trial on the existing Dayflow store.



If you demonstrate:

• Strong judgment

• Clean execution

• Creative leadership

• Performance improvement



You will be considered for:

• Long-term ownership of Dayflow

• Additional stores and budgets

• Expanded strategic role



?



How to Apply (Mandatory)



Applications without the following will be ignored.

1. Short intro (who you are, what you actually do)

2. 1–2 examples of brands/products you’ve owned end-to-end

3. Briefly explain:

• How you would audit a “working but underperforming” store

4. Answer this clearly (no fluff):



What usually kills stores that get traffic but don’t scale — and how do you personally fix it?



We care far more about thinking quality than credentials.

, social, packaging, and content.

Write 2 ad copies (primary text + headline) for a “heated eye massager” angle: (a) headache relief vibe, (b) screen fatigue vibe (no medical claims)

We are not looking for an ad button-pusher.



We are looking for one operator who can take full ownership of:

• Customer angles

• Creative direction

• Copywriting

• Competitor ad analysis

• Campaign setup, launch, and iteration



This starts as a paid trial using an existing store.

If you perform, this becomes long-term ownership across multiple brands.



If you need:

• Hand-holding

• Preset SOPs

• Someone else to “approve everything”



Do not apply.



?



Context



We operate a branded wellness ecommerce store called Dayflow.

• 15–20 generic but validated wellness products

(massagers, eye masks, recovery tools, comfort products)

• Store is functional:

• Pages are acceptable

• Funnels work

• Traffic flows correctly



What’s missing:

• Sharp customer angles

• Strong creative leadership

• Cohesive messaging

• A single owner accountable for performance



This role exists to fix that gap.



?



Your Responsibility (End-to-End Ownership)



1. Full Store & Funnel Audit



You will independently:

• Audit the store end-to-end

• Identify:

• Messaging gaps

• Angle mismatches

• Product prioritization mistakes

• Conversion friction

• D
:

• What to keep

• What to change

• What to ignore



You will implement changes, not just suggest them.



?



2. Customer Angles & Positioning



You must:

• Define real buyer avatars

• Map:

• Pain ? mechanism ? outcome

• Assign specific angles to specific products

• Kill weak products or angles if needed



Generic “busy professionals / self-care” thinking will be rejected.



?



3. Creative Direction + Production



You fully own creative execution:

• Find and analyze competitor ads (Meta / TikTok)

• Either:

• Edit creatives yourself

• OR create clear creative briefs and hooks that convert

• D
:

• Hooks

• Formats

• Proof mechanisms

• CTA style



You may also 1 to 1 swipe the ads for testing



You do not need to film UGC,

but you must know how to direct it precisely.



?



4. Copywriting (Required)



You will write:

• Ad copy

• Primary text

• Headlines

• Angle-specific messaging



If you outsource copy in your process, do not apply.

We need someone who understands copy as part of the system, not a handoff.



?



5. Paid Ads Execution



You will:

• Set up clean testing structures

• Launch campaigns (Meta primary; TikTok optional bonus)

• Iterate based on signal

• Kill losers decisively

• Communicate performance clearly



This is operator logic, not dashboard theater.



?



What We’re Looking For (Non-Negotiable)

• Proven experience running DTC / dropshipping ads

• Strong creative intuition + analytical judgment

• Comfortable making decisions without approval loops

• Able to own results, not just tasks

• Clear communicator who can explain why something works or fails



?



What This Is NOT

• ? Not a beginner role

• ? Not “just media buying”

• ? Not a freelancer who waits for instructions

• ? Not someone who avoids accountability



If you’ve never been the final decision-maker on a store, this is not for you.



?



Trial ? Long-Term Path



This engagement begins as a paid trial on the existing Dayflow store.



If you demonstrate:

• Strong judgment

• Clean execution

• Creative leadership

• Performance improvement



You will be considered for:

• Long-term ownership of Dayflow

• Additional stores and budgets

• Expanded strategic role



?

title: COO / Operations Integrator for High-Volume Dropshipping System
description:
Compensation & Incentives first month probationary period. (100 dollar increase to base everymonth thereafter)

This role is designed for someone who wants to prove themselves and earn based on performance.



Apply here: 



Base Retainer: $50 USD / week (Guaranteed for meeting minimum responsibilities).



Performance Bonus: $200 USD for every store that validates.





The Opportunity

I am building a data-driven, high-volume product validation machine, and I am looking for a COO-style Operations Integrator to own the execution.



We run a lean, volume-first testing model. We don’t test 3–5 products a month; we test 40–50. We use a structured multi-phase framework (P1 ? P2 ? P3) with clear gates, risk caps, and kill rules.



The machine is built. We need someone to fly it.



You will be the bridge between the founder and the specialist pods (research, creatives, media buying, sourcing). Your job is simple to define but hard to execute: Turn a proven validation model into a predictable, weekly execution rhythm.



Who You Are

You are NOT a VA ticking boxes. You are an operator.



You are an "Integrator": You are the calm operations brain that turns strategy into checklists, timelines, and ownership.



You think in systems: You naturally map how work moves from A ? B ? C and lock in that path.



You are data-literate: You don’t need to be a data scientist, but you can look at a report and instantly say, “We should kill this” or “We should keep testing this.”



You love upside: You prefer a role where your income scales with your results, rather than a flat hourly rate.



Core Responsibilities

You will own the day-to-day orchestration of the entire validation machine.



1. Pipeline & Capacity Management



Maintain a clear product pipeline: Idea ? Ready to Test ? P1 ? P2 ? P3 ? Scale.



Ensure we always have enough products in staging to hit our 40–50/month target.



Balance team capacity so researchers, creatives, and media buyers are never idle but never overloaded.



2. Process & SOP Execution



Enforce the 3-phase gate system. Ensure every product has the required assets before launch.



Confirm kill rules and budgets are respected (prevent emotional overspend).



Create and refine SOPs: Research handoffs, creative briefs, and media buying logging.



3. Cross-Team Coordination



Run regular check-ins with Research, Creative, and Media Buying pods.



Remove bottlenecks fast: If creatives are delayed, you escalate. If a buyer hasn’t launched, you chase. If quality drops, you reset expectations.



4. Systems & Data Hygiene



Create and own the Notion / ClickUp / Sheets structure as the single source of truth.



Maintain clean data for Products, Creatives, and Adsets so we can track validation rates and calculate bonuses accurately.



5. Continuous Improvement



Spot inefficiencies (e.g., "We need a better creative brief") and implement fixes.



Refine the validation model over time based on real results, not theory.



Requirements

2+ Years Experience: Operations, Project Management, or Integrator roles (ideally in E-commerce/Dropshipping).



Remote Team Management: Experience coordinating different pods (researchers, editors, media buyers).



Tech Stack: Strong proficiency in Notion, ClickUp/Asana, Google Sheets, and Slack.



Marketing Knowledge: Familiarity with ROAS, CPA, and AOV (you don’t need to be a media buyer, but you must speak the language).



Communication: Strong written English and relentless follow-through.



Definition: A store validates when it passes our internal criteria and moves into proper scaling.



The Upside: Since we test 40–50 products a month, the validation volume is significantly higher than low-volume models. If you run the system well, multiple validations per month are realistic, meaning your bonus will quickly outgrow the base.



Growth: As the system proves itself, we are open to discussing higher base pay and deeper profit-share arrangements.



How to Apply

Read this carefully. Applications that do not follow these instructions will be ignored.



Step 1: Review the full application details here:



Join 
 Application Channel



View Role Details on Notion



Step 2: Submit your proposal with the following:



Attention to Detail: Include the word “Integrator” somewhere in the first sentence of your proposal.



Experience: Share 1–2 specific examples where you acted as an integrator/ops manager in e-commerce. What was the monthly product or campaign volume?



Systems: Describe one system or workflow you built that made a tea
aningfully more efficient. What tools did you use? How did you measure improvement?



Scenario: If you joined tomorrow and we needed to reliably test 40–50 products per month, how would you structure the pipeline, communication, and weekly check-ins?



Confirmation: Confirm you understand and accept the compensation structure (Base + Performance Bonus).

title: Niche Shopify Store Builder (Brand-Focused)
description:
Niche Shopify Store Builder (Brand-Focused)

Remote | Part-Time | Contract



We are looking for a Niche Store Builder to build our premium brand store: DriveOn OS – Athlete-Grade Wellness. This role focuses on long-term branding and quality, not rapid testing stores.



Role: Niche Store Builder

Hours: 40 hrs/month (10 hrs/week)

Rate: USD $3/hour (USD $120/month)

Payment: Monthly via PayPal (1st of each month)

Start Date: ASAP



Scope:

• Build ONE high-quality branded Shopify store per month

• Focus on premium design, cohesion, and conversion

• Work with product researcher & project coordinator



Growth:

• Month 3+: 60–80 hrs/month ($180–$240)

• Month 6+: Second brand store project

• Revenue-share potential



PERFORMANCE-BASED COMPENSATION (KPI SYSTEM)



Monthly Earning Potential:

• Minimum: ~$240/month (foundation KPIs, pre-validation phase)

• Typical: ~$350–$550/month (Weeks 1–8)

• High Performance: Up to ~$840/month as store scales



How You Earn (Weekly KPIs – 7 total):

• Foundation KPIs (3): Consistency tasks, ~$50/week total

• Performance KPIs (3): Core role metrics, ~$190/week total

• Excellence KPI (1): Stretch goal, ~$75–$120/week



Caps:

• Weekly Cap: $250

• Monthly Cap: $1,000

(Excess goes to backlog, paid as revenue allows)



Revenue Rule:

All team payouts are capped at 15% of net weekly revenue.

If exceeded, earnings are pro-rated, with the remainder added to backlog.



Bonuses (Optional):

• Individual “Home Run” bonus: $100–$500

• Team bonus: 1–3% of team pool

(Not guaranteed, performance-based)



Payment:

• Weekly (paid Mondays for prior week)

• Via Payoneer, Wise, or agreed platform

• KPI report due Sunday 11:59 PM EST



Trial:

• 48–72 hrs unpaid pre-screening

• Required before formal engagement





Apply or send your application to:

title: Product Researcher (E-Commerce / Dropshipping)
description:
Summary

Full Deliverables, SOP, Templates for Job Posting. Please Read.







Must have own Product Research Tools: Sell the trend, Dropship IO, Winning Hunter, Helium 10, Jungle Scout, Auto DS etc.



Total Compensation: $70



DriveOn is launching a flagship branded dropshipping store selling athlete-grade wellness tools (massage guns, foam rollers, recovery equipment, mobility tools, supplements, home fitness equipment, and sport-specific gear) paired with professional training protocols.



We need an experienced Product Researcher to identify, validate, and curate the final product lineup that will make or break the store launch.



WHAT YOU'LL DELIVER

Milestone 1: Product Discovery & Validation ($15)

Output: 8 final hero SKUs, fully specified and ready to list



Research and identify products across all DriveOn categories (Recover, Mobility, Fitness, Golf IQ, Nutrition) that meet strict criteria:



Deliverables:



Product Master Sheet with all 8 SKUs



Supplier information (direct URLs, ratings, warehouse locations)



Price range diversity (entry, mid, premium, anchor products)



Cross-category representation



Backup supplier options for each product



Verify all margins meet 50%+ minimum (65-75% target)



Milestone 2: Competitive Intelligence ($12)

Output: Competitive analysis showing DriveOn's market positioning and differentiation strategy



Analyze top 10 branded dropshipping competitors to understand pricing strategies, bundle tactics, positioning approaches, and market gaps.



Milestone 3: Supplier Sourcing & Product Details ($15)

Output: Complete product specifications, descriptions, protocols, and SEO keywords for all 8 SKUs



For each of the 8 hero SKUs, compile comprehensive information ready for store listing.



Deliverables:



Product descriptions (
words, Problem-Solution-Outcome framework)



Complete specifications (dimensions, weight, materials, variants, what's included)



Pricing analysis (competitive benchmarks, margin calculations verified)



Competitive advantages per product (3-5 USPs)



SEO strategy (primary keyword + 3-5 long-tail keywords, meta descriptions)



Protocol outlines (5-10 steps, tracking methods, research citations)



Visual assets checklist (5-7 images per product, alt text for SEO)



Milestone 4: Bundle Architecture & Pricing ($18)

Output: 4-6 signature bundles with complete positioning, pricing, and margin analysis



Design bundle products that increase Average Order Value by 30-50% while maintaining 50%+ margins.



Deliverables:



4-6 signature bundles with strategic compositions



Bundle pricing strategy (12-18% discount, 50%+ margin verification)



Bundle positioning statements (who it's for, problem solved, outcomes)



Digital bonuses per bundle (protocols, templates, guides)



Bundle margin analysis (COGS, packaging, shipping, profit margins)



Bundle image concepts (for photographer/designer)



Competitive bundle analysis (how DriveOn bundles differentiate)



Website placement strategy recommendations



Milestone 5: Brand Alignment & Final Deliverable ($10)

Output: Complete, submission-ready research deliverable with quality verification and brand alignment confirmation



Ensure all research outputs align with DriveOn brand identity, mission, and ICP. Compile everything into one comprehensive template.



Deliverables:



Complete Research Deliverable Template filled out (all sections)



Brand alignment verification (mission, values, ICP fit confirmation)



Final quality checklist (80+ verification items, all marked complete)



Google Drive asset library organized and accessible (images, protocols, competitor screenshots)



Submission package with all required files and links



Researcher notes and recommendations for Store Builder/Lister



REQUIRED SKILLS & EXPERIENCE

Essential:



? Product research experience (ecommerce, dropshipping, or retail)



? Proficiency with Google Sheets, Google Drive, Google Docs



? Dropshipping platform experience (CJ Dropshipping, AutoDS, Spocket)



? Experience with product research tools (Sell The Trend, Helium 10, etc.)



? Fitness/wellness/athletic industry knowledge



? E-commerce brand building experience



? SEO keyword research experience



? Bundle strategy or merchandising experience



? Competitive analysis experience



HOW YOU'LL WORK

Deliverables-Based:

Each milestone is a specific, measurable deliverable. Complete the milestone, get paid. No hourly tracking, no time sheets. You own your schedule.



Milestone 1 Complete: $15 paid

Milestone 2 Complete: $12 paid

Milestone 3 Complete: $15 paid

Milestone 4 Complete: $18 paid

Milestone 5 Complete: $10 paid



Total: $70



What makes a product "brandable" vs. generic?

How would you evaluate if a product has protocol integration potential?

Describe your approach to finding underutilized products competitors are missing

title: Corporate Travel Consultant
description:
Corporate Travel Consultant (Remote – Philippines)

Location: Remote – Philippines

Arrangement: Full-time, 40 hours per week



About the Role



We are seeking an experienced Corporate Travel Consultant to support non-client-facing corporate travel operations. This role will focus on corporate booking assistance, queue management, and back-office travel administration, while client-facing consultants remain in-house.



The successful candidate will work closely with the internal team and provide operational support to ensure travel bookings and administrative processes are handled accurately and efficiently.



Key Responsibilities



Corporate Travel Support

- Assist with travel bookings and reservations where required

- Prepare and manage booking records within GDS systems

Prepare accurate corporate travel quotations when requested

Manage and monitor travel booking queues

Process fulfilments, amendments, reissues, and cancellations

Generate and transact from basic operational or booking reports to support the internal team (including uninvoiced, unreceipted fees, and bookings status reports)

Ensure compliance with client travel policies

Maintain booking accuracy and documentation standards



Back-Office Administration



Reconcile booking financials

Manage supplier confirmations and ticketing follow-ups

Support workflow tracking via 
 (or internal systems)

Update CRM systems (if applicable)

Maintain detailed records of transactions and communications

Assist with reporting and operational documentation



Systems & Tools



Work within internal travel and operational systems, including (but not limited to):

Tramada (where applicable)

Amadeus


 (task tracking)

HubSpot CRM



Required Skills & Experience



2+ years of experience in corporate travel or travel administration

Strong quoting and fare construction knowledge

Experience managing booking queues

High attention to detail and accuracy

Experience using travel systems – Amadeus (GDS and mid-office systems preferred)

Familiarity with Tramada is highly desirable

Comfortable working in a non-client-facing support capacity

Strong English writing and communication skills

Ability to work independently in a remote setup

title: Bodycam Footage Researcher for Youtube Channel
description:

Responsibilities:



Research and find New body cam footage from police cases in the USA



Contact the police department or appropriate source to request access to footage



Use professional, well-written
s (we can provide templates via ChatGPT)



Track all cases in a clear spreadsheet/database



Deliver daily research updates

Strong communication and attention to detail



Ability to work independently and meet deadlines

$20 per footage found



*Extra pay if you have previous experience in sourcing or know the process professionally!!*

title: Experienced B2B Telemarketer
description:
* PLEASE READ THE ENTIRE DESCRIPTION BEFORE APPLYING *





IMPORTANT:



- We are ONLY looking for candidates with previous B2B cold calling experience.

- If you have only worked warm leads, inbound calls, or appointment setting from ads, do NOT apply.



COMPANY INFO:



- My name is Jordan. I am the founder of a fast-growing company that helps small businesses never miss a call using an A.I. Receptionist.

- This is a simple, high-demand service that business owners immediately understand. We are looking for 1–2 strong cold callers to help introduce it to small business owners across the U.S.

- We are not a large corporate call center.  We are a small team with an owner focussed on helping the team get real results.



WHAT YOU WILL DO:



- Source your own B2B leads

- Cold call small business owners

- Book them for a free consultation

- Ensure they show up to the call

- That's it!  You do not close deals. Our closing team handles everything, your only job is to get them to show-up.



MUST HAVE (DO NOT APPLY IF THIS DOES NOT DESCRIBE YOU):



- Proven B2B cold calling experience

- Strong English with no heavy accent

- Reliable internet and a quiet workspace

- Comfortable making high-volume outbound calls

- Put the phrase “Dial And Smile” in your subject line so we know you read this



COMPENSATION:



-$2/hour base 

-$25 per attended show-up

- $100 per closed deal

- Monthly performance bonuses



HOURS:



- Part-time (20 hours per week) with the option to transition to full time

- Schedule: 2:00 AM – 6:00 AM Philippine Time



HOW TO APPLY:



- Do not send a generic message.

- In your application, include 2 things: Your specific B2B cold calling experience AND why this role fits you

- Do not use chat GPT or similar responses, those will be rejected.  We read each application and looking for a genuine person to reach out.

title: Meta ad creative for local business
description:
We run
 and Instagram ads for local service businesses such as:

 • Landscaping companies

 • Cleaning companies

 • Gutter cleaning companies

 • Personal trainers

 • Training programs



We are looking for a creative editor who can produce Meta ad creatives quickly, including video ads, image ads, and AI-assisted content.



This role can turn into ongoing work if the first projects go well.



?



Immediate Task



We are looking for someone who can start right away.



There are a few ad creatives that need to be produced within the next 12–15 hours. If the work is strong and turnaround is fast, this will likely lead to regular creative work moving forward.



?



What You’ll Be Doing



You will create
 and Instagram ad creatives, including:

title: Operations Manager – Telemedicine & Digital Document Services (Full-Time, Remote)

title: Credit Repair Specialist
description:
About the Role



We are seeking an experienced Credit Repair Specialist Virtual Assistant to support our growing credit education and consulting business. This role requires hands on experience with credit disputes, compliance based processes, and client file management. This is not an entry level role.



The ideal candidate understands U.S. credit reporting systems, Fair Credit Reporting Act processes, and has direct experience working inside Credit Repair Cloud and submitting CFPB complaints.



Key Responsibilities



Process and manage credit dispute cases using Credit Repair Cloud



Submit and manage CFPB complaints for non response or violations



Review credit reports for inaccuracies, unverifiable items, and compliance issues



Track dispute timelines and follow up on responses

title: Virtual Assistant – Sales Outreach, Guest & Travel Planner
description:
We are proud to operate two complementary businesses based in SW Michigan, USA:

Kohoe, a vacation and event-rental facility offering tented events, lodging, and unique guest experiences. (airbnb)



True Love Roses, a floral business specializing in premium roses and B2B/retail flower delivery. (weekly flower delivery company)

You will serve as a key support for both: helping drive new business for the flower company, managing guest relations for the camp, and coordinating personal/travel logistics for the floral business and owners and guests who opt for our trip planning service.



Key Responsibilities

TRUE LOVE ROSES

Sales Outreach & New Accounts acquisition and products (True Love Roses):

Research, identify and prospect new corporate, event-planner and repeat-order accounts for the flower business.

Craft and send outreach
s, follow-up messages, track status in CRM, and convert leads into clients.

Maintain lead pipeline, update CRM regularly, and report conversion metrics.

Create CRM ( currently use Apollo)



Kohoe

Guest & Booking Management (Camp Kohoe):

Respond to guest enquiries for bookings, coordinate with cleaning/maintenance/vendors, manage calendars and availability.

Handle guest messaging (pre-stay, during stay, post-stay), upsell event tent/tables-chairs rentals when relevant.

Assist with check-in/out logistics and guest follow-up to build repeat business.



Travel & Personal Logistics

Plan and book flights, hotels, ground transport, create itineraries for business travel associated with the floral business or Airbnb



CRM & Reporting:

Ensure data accuracy and up-to-date segmentation.

Provide weekly reports summarizing: outreach activity (number contacted, responses, conversions), guest bookings for the camp, travel plan status and upcoming tasks.

Use Google Sheets/CRM tools, Trello/Asana (or similar) for task management, and communicate via Slack/Zoom/Teams.



Ideal Candidate

Based in the Philippines with reliable internet connection, dedicated workspace and good English verbal/written communication.

Proven experience in: sales outreach or lead generation, guest/booking management or hospitality, and travel logistics coordination.

Comfortable using CRM systems, Google Workspace (Sheets, Docs, Drive), spreadsheet tracking, and task management tools.

Highly organized, proactive, able to multitask across different business lines (floral + hospitality + travel).

Flexible for US ET overlap: able to respond during key hours and occasionally handle urgent tasks outside regular hours.

Results-oriented: able to track KPIs, clearly communicate progress and issues, and propose improvements.





Application Instructions



Please submit:

Your resume/CV.

A short video intro of yourself and relevant experience less then 1 min

A cover letter: include a brief case/example of sales/outreach you’ve done, guest/booking management or hospitality support you’ve handled, and travel arrangements you’ve managed (if any).

Your availability overlapping US ET hours. We would like a

Your expected monthly salary in USD for ~ 40 hours/week.

Are you currently working with other businesses?

What are 3 recent professional successes?



We’re proud of the businesses we’ve built and are building — rooted in creativity, hospitality, and integrity — and we’re looking for someone who shares those values. If you’re ready to grow with a stable, supportive team that rewards initiative and long-term commitment, we’d love to meet you. Apply today and take the next step toward joining the Kohoe and True Love Roses family.

title: Full-Time Video Editor — James Jani Style YouTube Documentaries (NBA Content)
description:
NBA Insider (
) is a YouTube channel producing long-form NBA documentary-style video essays. We are hiring a full-time video editor to join us long-term with consistent, ongoing work.



=== THE STYLE WE'RE AFTER ===



Our #1 reference is James Jani: 

Watch any of his videos before applying. That is the quality and style we are going for.



Additional style references:

- Vox: 

- Johnny Harris: 



In short: cinematic, documentary-grade video essays with strong pacing, heavy motion graphics, and narrative tension. Think Netflix-level sports documentaries adapted for YouTube.



If you watch a James Jani video and think "I can edit like that" -- keep reading.



=== WHAT YOU WILL BE DOING ===



- Editing documentary-style NBA video essays (minimum 15 minutes per video) from voiceover, research docs, and creative briefs

- Sourcing all visuals yourself: archival NBA footage, game highlights, press photos, Getty Images, high-quality images, and any relevant media using fair use principles

- Creating motion graphics, animated text, kinetic typography, data visualizations, lower thirds, and transitions (this is a MAJOR part of the role -- not optional)

- Color grading and sound design to broadcast/cinematic standard

- Pacing and structuring edits to maintain narrative tension and viewer retention throughout the full video

- Working directly with the channel owner on creative direction for each video



Note: We do NOT provide raw footage. Your job is to source all visuals (B-roll, archival clips, images, photos) from the internet using fair use. You should be experienced in finding and curating high-quality visual material for documentary-style content.



=== MOTION GRAPHICS REQUIREMENT ===



This is non-negotiable. You MUST be highly skilled in After Effects (or equivalent motion graphics software). You need to be comfortable building from scratch: custom animations, kinetic text, data-driven visuals, dynamic transitions, and animated infographics. If motion graphics is not your strength, this role is not for you.



=== WHAT WE PROVIDE ===



- Artlist Max subscription (music, SFX, stock footage)

- Epidemic Sound subscription

- Voiceover audio for each video

- Research materials and scripts

- Clear creative briefs with references for each video

- Long-term, consistent work (we publish regularly)



=== REQUIRED SKILLS ===



- Expert-level video editing (Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or DaVinci Resolve)

- Strong motion graphics and animation (After Effects or equivalent) -- NON-NEGOTIABLE

- Ability to source high-quality visuals, archival footage, and images independently using fair use

- Color grading and sound design

- Pacing and storytelling instinct (you know how to build tension and hold attention for 15+ minutes)

- Reliable high-speed internet connection and a capable editing workstation (you will be handling large project files)

- Strong English communication (written)



=== NICE TO HAVE ===



- Thumbnail design

- 3D motion graphics

- DaVinci Resolve proficiency

- Experience editing sports or documentary content

- Behance, personal website, or Vimeo profile with your work



=== COMPENSATION ===



25,500 PHP per video (equivalent to $600 CAD / $440 USD per video)



Payment is per completed video, paid promptly upon delivery. The more videos you complete, the more you earn. If your work is strong and consistent, there is no shortage of projects -- we publish regularly and are growing.



This is a long-term position with steady, ongoing work -- not a one-off gig.



=== TURNAROUND ===



Maximum 10 days per completed video. Ideal turnaround is 7 days. This includes weekends -- we operate on a delivery-based schedule, not a 9-to-5.



=== HOW TO APPLY ===



To be considered, you MUST include ALL of the following in your application. Incomplete applications will be ignored.



1. A link to your portfolio, showreel, Behance, Vimeo, or personal website -- specifically any documentary-style, James Jani-style, or motion graphics work you have done

2. Your best 60-second edit or reel that demonstrates your motion graphics and pacing ability

3. A short message (2-3 sentences): what editing software do you use, and what do you think makes James Jani's editing style effective?

4. Your expected rate per video in PHP



Applications without a portfolio link will NOT be reviewed.



=== WHAT HAPPENS AFTER YOU APPLY ===



Step 1: We review your portfolio and reel

Step 2: If your work matches what we are looking for, we will reach out to discuss the role

Step 3: Our top 2-3 candidates will be asked to complete a short PAID test edit (paid at a reduced rate) before final hiring



=== FAQ (READ BEFORE APPLYING) ===



Q: Is this a one-time project?

A: No. This is a long-term, full-time role with consistent work. The more videos you deliver, the more you earn.



Q: Do you provide raw footage?

A: No. You are responsible for sourcing all visuals (archival footage, game clips, photos, images) using fair use. We provide voiceover, scripts, research, and creative briefs.



Q: What software should I use?

A: Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or DaVinci Resolve for editing. After Effects (or equivalent) for motion graphics.



Q: How will I receive project files?

A: We will share voiceover, scripts, and briefs via Google Drive.



Q: How will I get paid?

A: Via Payoneer, PayPal, or Wise -- whichever you prefer. Paid per video upon delivery.



Q: What is the expected turnaround per video?

A: Maximum 10 days per video. Ideal is 7 days.



Q: Do I need to be available during specific hours?

A: No fixed hours. We care about quality and meeting deadlines. That said, you should be responsive on messaging during reasonable hours for communication.



Q: How long are the videos?

A: Minimum 15 minutes. Most videos will be 15-25 minutes.

title: TikTok Budget Content Creator — Screen Recording + Video Editing
description:
I run a budgeting content brand on TikTok. I create short-form videos where I break down someone’s monthly budget (income, bills, expenses, debt, savings) while showing the numbers being entered into a budgeting app on screen.



I need someone to handle two things for each video:



1. Screen recording: Open the budgeting app on your computer (web version), follow the budget table I provide, and record yourself entering the numbers into the app. Slowly, cleanly, no mistakes. Use OBS or any screen recorder.



2. Video editing: Take the screen recording, the voiceover audio I provide, and the hook videos I provide. Cut and arrange the hook (~7 seconds), then the budget screen recording synced to the voiceover. Put it all together into a final TikTok-ready video using CapCut (or similar).



Everything you need for each video will be organized in a shared Google Drive folder. You pick up the materials, do the work, and upload the finished video back to the Drive. Simple and repeatable.



What You Need

    • A computer that can screen record clearly (OBS or similar — smooth, no lag, good resolution)

    • Experience with CapCut or similar short-form video editing tools

    • Attention to detail — numbers must be entered exactly as provided

    • Reliable internet and access to Google Drive

    • Available to complete 3–5 videos per day on a consistent basis



What I Provide For Each Video

    • The full budget story (written text)

    • A budget table with exact numbers to enter into the app

    • Voiceover audio (pre-recorded)

    • Hook videos for the opening of the video (~7 seconds of character footage)

    • Access to the budgeting app (test account provided)

    • Reference videos so you know exactly how the final product should look



Pay

Starting pay is $0.60 per finished video. This is ongoing, part-time work. The more consistent and reliable you are, the more videos you get. Payment via PayPal, once per month.



How to Apply



Please include in your application:

    • A short screen recording sample from your computer (can be anything — just show me the quality)

    • An example of a short video you’ve edited (TikTok, Reels, Shorts — anything short-form)

    • How many videos per day you can realistically handle



Do NOT apply if you are going to copy-paste a generic cover letter. I will ignore it.

Aisiki Group is a growing property company specialising in serviced accommodation, long-term lets, and HMO management. We provide high-quality housing solutions to contractors, corporate clients, insurance companies, and relocation agencies. As we expand, we are seeking a motivated Lead Generation Specialist to drive business growth through targeted prospecting and pipeline management for our Service Accommodation business.

https://www.aisiki.co.uk/

You will use a mix of technology, data-driven research, and communication skills to open doors with decision-makers and help us maximise occupancy and direct bookings.

? Identify and research potential clients (contractors, relocation agencies, corporate housing teams, insurance companies, etc.)

Must Haves

title: Customer Experience Virtual Assistant (Phone Based)
description:
Note: Interested applicants must submit their application exclusively through the OnlineJobs platform. Do not
 the company directly. Any candidate who contacts the company outside of the OnlineJobs system will be disqualified from consideration.



Are you someone who takes pride in handling customer interactions the right way—calmly, professionally, and consistently? Do you do well with clear expectations, scripts, and processes, and enjoy improving quickly through feedback? If so, Maid In America Cleaning Services, based in Clarksville, TN, wants to hear from you.



We’re seeking a Customer Experience Virtual Assistant to serve as a frontline point of contact for our customers and a dependable executor of structured processes. Working closely with our sales and customer experience teams, you’ll be responsible for answering inbound calls, handling customer requests, following defined scripts, and routing issues accurately.



This role is process-driven and phone-heavy, especially during training. The work can be fast-paced at times, but it is highly structured. Your ability to stay calm, follow instructions precisely, and take coaching seriously will have a direct impact on customer satisfaction and team efficiency.



What You’ll Do



- Answer & Handle Inbound Customer Calls: Be the first point of contact for customers and new inquiries, answering calls promptly, professionally, and calmly while following provided phone scripts.

- Follow Scripts & Decision Trees: Handle common customer needs—such as scheduling questions, reschedules, basic service inquiries, and initial issue intake—by following clear scripts and escalation rules exactly.

- Route Requests Accurately: Determine when a request can be handled directly and when it should be escalated to the appropriate tea
mber, ensuring clean handoffs and no dropped balls.

- Create a Positive Customer Experience: Make customers feel heard, cared for, and confident that their request is being handled, even when issues or complaints arise.

- Document Interactions & Updates: Log accurate notes and updates in our systems so the internal team always has clear context and next steps.

- Support Structured Administrative Workflows: Execute defined backend processes (task tracking, updates, file movement, and follow-through) by following SOPs and checklists without skipping steps.

- Receive Coaching & Improve Quickly: Actively apply feedback on calls, scripts, and workflows, improving speed, accuracy, and confidence over time.



Who We’re Looking For



- Process-Driven & Detail-Oriented: You’re comfortable following scripts, checklists, and SOPs exactly, and you don’t skip steps or “freestyle” your work.

- Calm & Professional on the Phone: You communicate clearly and confidently, stay composed with customers, and can handle questions or mild frustration without getting flustered.

- Highly Coachable: You take feedback seriously, apply it quickly, and improve fast—especially when it comes to phone scripts and customer handling.

- Reliable & Consistent: You show up on time, follow through on tasks, and can be counted on to do things the right way every time.

- Comfortable with Structure: You prefer clear expectations, defined processes, and knowing exactly what “good” looks like in your role.

- Tech-Comfortable: You’re comfortable working inside tools like Slack, shared documents, and task systems—or you can learn them quickly with instructions.

title: Brand & Content Designer (Remote, Full-Time)
description:
Job description



At Miraco Nutripharm, we are dedicated to advancing health and improving lives. Our goal is to relieve 1 billion people worldwide of pain and discomfort. If you know of anyone who has been worried about or suffered from any health problems, join us in making a positive impact by reducing the pain and distress others have to endure.



We’re seeking a full-time Brand and Content Designer to lead the creative direction of our brand across packaging, offline marketing, and digital platforms. This role is ideal for a strategic, hands-on creative who can own both design and content production, from ideation to execution, while supporting our expansion across Southeast Asia and global markets.



As a key member of the team, you'll be responsible for producing compelling visuals and content that reflect the heart of our brand and connect meaningfully with consumers, healthcare professionals, and distribution partners.



Key Responsibilities:



-Brand Development & Creative Direction-

*Lead development of visual identity for new and existing brands, including logos, packaging, brand guidelines, typography and brand story

*Propose and implement creative directions from Pantone color palettes to iconography, font systems and photography guidelines

*Establish and maintain consistent branding across all physical and digital touchpoints, while ensuring all brand assets are stored and updated in an organized, easily accessible database



-Graphic & Marketing Design-

*Design high-impact materials such as product packaging, brochures, point-of-sale displays, in-store visuals and Continuing Medical Education (CME) event collaterals

*Develop pitch decks, sales presentations and brand kits for internal and external stakeholders

*Create visuals for promotions, campaigns, and trade shows for greater awareness and conversion



-Content Creation & Copywriting-

*Collaborate with the team to develop content ideas and messaging for various platforms (digital ads, social media, web,
)

*Write and edit marketing copy as needed, including taglines, brand messaging, product descriptions, and promotional materials

*Contribute to storytelling, campaign ideation, consumer education initiatives, and customer loyalty initiatives



-Digital & Multimedia Content-

*Produce digital assets for ecommerce platforms, social media ads, newsletters, and display ads (Google Ads Meta, TikTok)

*Edit videos and animations for campaigns, tutorials, events, and educational content using tools like CapCut, Premiere Pro, or After Effects

*Repurpose content across multiple formats and languages when applicable



-Cross-Functional Collaboration-

*Work closely with marketing, sales, pharmacists and leadership to turn strategic goals into compelling creative output

*Participate in product launch discussions and brand development brainstorming sessions

*Coordinate with external freelancers or vendors as needed



-Regional Support & Market Expansion-

*Adapt assets for regional markets including Singapore, Malaysia, Mainland China & Hong Kong and future expansion areas

*Ensure consistency across regions while accounting for local cultural, language, market and regulatory differences



-Requirements-

*Minimum 3–5 years of experience in graphic design, brand development, content creation, or multimedia production

*Strong portfolio showcasing design, branding, and content/copywriting capabilities across print and digital

*Proficient in Adobe Creative Suite (Illustrator, Photoshop, Premiere Pro), CapCut, Canva, Figma, or similar tools

*Excellent understanding of branding, packaging, digital marketing, and customer communication

*Creative thinker with strong attention to detail and an eye for aesthetic balance

*Highly organized, self-driven, and able to manage multiple timelines in a fast-paced environment

*Strong communication and collaboration skills, especially in remote work settings

*Experience in the health, wellness, pharmaceutical, or consumer goods sector is a plus

*Degree in Design, Marketing, Communications, or a related field preferred but not mandatory



Kindly submit your updated CV and sample portfolio to 
 for our review.

title: Listing and Transactions Coordinator
description:
We are looking for a highly organized and detail-oriented MLS Listing Coordinator to handle the administrative management of our real estate listings. This role is responsible for processing listing documentation, coordinating photos and marketing materials, entering and maintaining accurate MLS listings, and managing showing logistics.



This is a full-time, long-term remote position with a growing U.S.-based real estate investment and brokerage team. We value reliability, precision, and proactive communication.



Key Responsibilities:

• Review and process listing documents such as listing agreements, disclosures, and addenda.

• Enter and manage listings in the MLS with strict attention to accuracy and compliance.

• Coordinate professional photography, lockbox installation, and showing instructions.

• Schedule and manage showings through MLS systems and showing platforms.

• Ensure all required documents are uploaded and meet brokerage and MLS compliance requirements.

• Communicate with agents, photographers, vendors, and internal tea
mbers.

• Assist with listing marketing coordination when needed.



Required Skills and Experience:

• Previous experience working with an MLS system.

• Strong understanding of U.S. real estate listing documentation and MLS compliance requirements.

• Excellent attention to detail with the ability to manage multiple listings and meet deadlines.

• Proficiency with Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Drive, Gmail).

• Strong written and verbal English communication skills.

• Ability to work full-time during U.S. business hours with a reliable internet connection.



Compensation and Benefits:

• Monthly Salary: $750

• 15 paid vacation days per year plus major U.S. federal holidays

• 13th Month Pay (paid in December)

• Health insurance coverage

• Long-term growth opportunities within an expanding real estate team



How to Apply:

In the first line of your application, write: “MLS Accuracy Is My Priority.”



Please also include:

• A summary of your experience working with U.S. real estate listings

• The MLS systems you have used

• A short paragraph explaining how you ensure accuracy when handling compliance paperwork

• Your internet speed test result and computer specifications.

title: Senior Bookkeeper *Superstar Only*
description:
We are looking for two experienced bookkeepers to join our finance team and take ownership of expense categorization, transaction reconciliation, accounts payable, and invoice processing across multiple legal entities.



The bookkeepers will work closely with the CFO, ensuring that all financial transactions are accurately recorded, categorized, and reconciled on a regular basis. This role is ideal for someone who is detail-oriented, organized, and comfortable managing financial data across multiple platforms — including Xero, Stripe, PayPal, Shopify, and our custom CRM.



Key Responsibilities

- Categorize all expenses and transactions in Xero, ensuring accurate classification for reporting and compliance

- Reconcile transactions across multiple payment platforms, including Stripe, PayPal, Shopify, and custom CRM tools

- Manage accounts payable, ensuring all invoices are properly reviewed, recorded, and scheduled for payment

- Assist with invoice processing, ensuring all vendor invoices are accurately documented and match purchase orders where applicable

- Work closely with the CFO to provide up-to-date financial records for monthly, quarterly, and annual reporting

- Ensure all financial data is accurate and properly organized to support compliance across multiple jurisdictions

- Flag discrepancies or unusual transactions for review by the CFO



What We’re Looking For

- At least 5 years of experience in bookkeeping and expense reconciliation

- Strong proficiency in Xero

- Experience working with Stripe, PayPal, Shopify, and custom CRMs

- Strong attention to detail and organizational skills

- Experience handling multi-entity, multi-currency financial data preferred

- Comfortable working in a remote-first environment

- Fluent in English, both written and spoken

Working Schedule

title: Trading Operations Supervisor - Prop Firm Traders
description:
**PLEASE NOTE** This position has been filled for the time being. There are though current open opportunities with our firm to join as one of our elite traders. Please see posting here:



https://www.onlinejobs.ph/jobseekers/job/Prop-Firm-Trader-FX-Indices-Crypto
Hello!



Our company is growing - I have built a diversified team of experienced and talented prop firm traders across multiple firms and platforms. Each trader works virtually on their own and are in the Philippines and supervised by one operations supervisor. I am now seeking another Trading Operations Supervisor to manage day-to-day virtual operations, enforce rules, oversee reporting, and actively manage a team of traders. This is also a virtual position where you will manage the traders via our 
, Zoom calls , virtual meetings and Google Doc tracking. 



This role combines operations, risk oversight, and people management. You will not place trades, but you will be responsible for ensuring traders operate professionally, follow rules, stay disciplined, and communicate effectively - all done remotely. 



Core Responsibilities:

 - Trading Operations & Risk Oversight

 - Maintain a centralized portfolio dashboard covering all traders and accounts

 - Track trader status (evaluation, funded, paused, reset, removed)

 - Ensure strict adherence to prop firm rules and drawdown limits

 - Review daily trade journals for completeness, logic, and discipline

 - Monitor risk exposure and potential correlation across traders

 - Identify early warning signs of rule fatigue or risky behavior

 - Produce weekly and monthly performance summaries for the Owner



Trader Management & Leadership:

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for traders on day-to-day matters

 - Set expectations for professionalism, communication, and reporting

 - Provide encouragement, structure, and accountability

 - Address trader concerns, frustration, and emotional stress constructively

 - Reinforce discipline during drawdowns and after winning streaks

 - Ensure traders understand and respect boundaries and rules

 - Escalate serious issues, violations, or recommendations to the Owner



Communication & Coordination:

 - Maintain clear, calm, and consistent communication with traders

 - Deliver direct feedback when expectations are not being met

 - Coordinate updates, reminders, and operational changes

 - Act as a buffer between traders and the owner to reduce noise



What You Will Not Do:

 - You will not place trades

 - You will not change or dictate trading strategies

 - You will not override prop firm rules

 - You will not promise compensation, bonuses, or profit shares



You manage people and process — not positions.



Requirements:

 - Strong understanding of trading concepts (FX, indices, or crypto)

 - Familiarity with prop firm environments and rule structures

 - Proven ability to manage people professionally

 - Excellent organizational and reporting skills

 - Strong written and verbal communication

 - Emotional intelligence and calm temperament

 - Comfortable enforcing rules and having difficult conversations



Strongly Preferred:

 - Prior experience with prop firm trading or trading teams

 - Experience reviewing trading journals or performance data

 - Operations, compliance, or risk-management background

 - Experience managing remote teams

 - Strong spreadsheet and dashboard skills



Work Schedule:

 - Flexible hours

 - Estimated 15–25 hours per week initially

 - Potential to grow into a larger, more senior role as the portfolio scales



Compensation:

- Fixed weekly salary 

- Possible discretionary performance bonus

- Long-term opportunity with a growing trading operation

(Compensation is not directly tied to trading profits to maintain objectivity.)



How to Apply:

Please include:

 - A short introduction

 - Relevant experience (trading, prop firms, operations, people management)

 - An example of how you would handle a trader breaking rules under pressure



Looking forward to hearing from you!

title: Cold Caller / Acquisition Specialist – Only Operators Apply
description:
Cold Caller / Acquisition Specialist – Only Operators Apply



READ THIS ENTIRE POST BEFORE APPLYING.

We are not looking for the average salesperson.

We do not reward effort, intent, or “personality.”

We hire and promote ONLY on documented results and system compliance.



Who We Are



We are a high-performance real estate investment company seeking a cold caller/acquisition specialist who thrives in a systemized, metrics-driven environment.

If you can’t handle daily tracking, clear quotas, strict process, and real consequences—you will not last here.



What You’ll Do



Make 100+ outbound calls daily to property owners and motivated seller lists using our scripts and dialers.



Qualify leads, set appointments, and advance prospects through our defined sales process—no improvising.



Log every activity in our CRM and submit a daily scorecard—no exceptions.



Handle objections professionally and convert “no” into opportunity, always within our guidelines.



Work a tightly scheduled, high-urgency day. If you don’t hit your numbers, you will be held accountable.



What Success Looks Like



5+ qualified leads generated daily



Consistent, documented attainment of weekly and monthly targets



100% adherence to scripts, CRM tracking, and process



Zero tolerance for drama, excuses, or off-script behavior



All communication clear, professional, and recorded as required



What You Need



Proven sales results—You must provide pay stubs, CRM screenshots, or manager references verifying quota attainment.



High call volume history (100+ calls/day in previous roles)



Comfort with strict accountability—if you miss, you fix it; if you fail repeatedly, you’re out.



Experience using sales dialers and CRMs—no learning curve.



Fluency in English; accent must be clear and build rapport with U.S. sellers



100% reliability—no missed days, no connectivity issues, no excuses.



How to Apply (Non-Negotiable)



Incomplete, late, or non-compliant applications will be ignored.



Voice Recording:

Submit a 2-minute audio file introducing yourself and reading this short sample script [link/script will be provided].



Proof of Performance:

Attach a recent pay stub, commission statement, CRM screenshot, or provide a direct manager reference confirming your results.



Written Response:

Answer, in writing:

a) What were your daily/weekly targets in your last role?

b) What happened if you missed quota?

c) How did you track and report your activity?



Subject Line Test:

Include the word “system” in your subject line when you submit your application.



If you are unwilling or unable to follow these instructions exactly, do not apply.

If you are tired of being dragged down by low performers and want to join a true operator’s team, this is your seat.

The system always wins. If you’re ready, prove it.



Apply now with all materials attached.

Only complete, documented, and on-time applications will be reviewed.



:
